What I also dislike is the reason why she decided to leave her family. Madison had an opportunity to show feminism in a positive light, but he instead showed an extremist. Right after she reads SCUM Manifesto this happens:

"Then one day I'm getting ready for school and she knocks on my door with a bag packed and she tells me she's going to live at something called Women's Land, where no one ever has to talk to men."

Of course. Here is evil feminism breaking up a perfectly good family. I supposed this is just as good a time to reveal my master plan. Ladies, are you ready take over the world, moving all men underground only to be used for breeding, whist women rule the world? Muahahahahahaha! We'll hold the world hostage for a million dollars!

The next section spoils the ending, so read only if you are burning with curiosity or rage. Either will do.

"Save us with your Mighty-Joe-Young Penis!"

The Girls are all bound to this little beach by a curse placed on them by their father for... reasons. I didn't really understand why this was, but I think it had something to do with seeking revenge on their mother. BTW, their dad is the Endlessness and their mother is the Deepness. Don't ask me what that means. Anyway, it's really not important. What's important is this curse because it's the reason for why the Girls are so sexual. The book has sections where the Girls narrate and they describe this "knife" they have. This supposed "knife" is basically good looks. Perky breasts, perfect butt, blond, overall hotness. This is another stereotype I picked up on where women, who approach men instead of waiting for a guy or use their looks to gain things, are looked at as "predators."

But, of course, when the summer ends the Girls go into some weird lethargic state where their hair skin become dull and their faces sullen. No boys, no "knife." So basically I pictured then like Ursula from The Little Mermaid.

The only way each one of them to break the curse is if they have sex with a virgin boy. And they can't even initiate the encounter. They have to wait for Sam to talk to them first. So let's recap here: Not only do the Girls not have a choice when it comes to breaking the curse (well, they kinda do: break it or die), but it must be done by a male penis swooping in to liberate them. Their sexuality is not their own. It is owned by men.

Excuse me for a moment.

*RAGE*

I mean, wow! I really think this book hit on almost every way to demean a women. That is quite a feat considering I never thought I'd read a book that offended me more than Fifty Shades of Grey . Congrats, September Girls! You get the new title of Worst Book I've Ever Read right up there next to Revealing Eden (and that's saying something).

If it isn't obvious, this book is terrible and I could never recommend it with a good conscious. But what do I know? Both Kirkus and Publisher's Weekly thought it was brilliant and gave it glowing reviews. Clearly, this is the sign of the end of the world because here's the truth: Reading September Girls was like being swept away by the ocean and drowni--

CLEAR!

ARC was received via publisher for an honest review. No monies or favors were exchanged, though, I guess that's pretty obvious.

More reviews and other fantastical things at Cuddlebuggery Book Blog.
rows.title: I could never recommend this book with a good conscious...

rows.text:
SPOILERS
The Replacement by Brenna Yovanoff follows a sixteen year old Mackie who is a changeling who was swapped for the real son of the Doyle family. There's this unspoken agreement in the town of Gentry that a child is stolen every seven years, replaced, and well, killed for the prosperity of the town. Or something like that. Honestly, I have no idea. I'm just trying to remember what it said on the cover.

I decided to check this book out because it was recommended to me and it was supposed to be a darker faerie book. I love reading faerie books and darker books as well, so I figured I'd give it a shot. That was a waste.

I have a feeling this review might turn into a rant...

So, the story starts out with Mackie lusting over the hot wannabe tramp girl. After finding out this random girl, Tate, lost her sister, he sees hooker girl (whatever her name is) coming to him after giving blood. Blood happens to make him go weak at the knees, sick and possibly faint. So he leaves. Before he can tell hooker girl he has the hots for her. Even though it's pretty sure she likes him and they could go out, he has to be invisible.

Think I'm kidding about hooker girl being a hooker? "'Tis the season for girls to dress like hookers." So... apparently, it's okay to not only dress up like a hooker, but be known as such as well. I'm not saying it doesn't happen, I'm not stupid or in some protective bubble. I know people do dress like a hooker, but it's the fact that it's degrading is what annoys me. There's no point in having this in a book because it adds nothing to the plot or characters. If the girl was described as a hooker because she works nights because that's the only way to get money and some tragic story that actually meant something to the story, then fine. Go ahead and do that. But to have it in there just to turn guys on, that's stupid and pointless.

Back to the story, so Mackie gets sick from blood and metal, yet he plays instruments. Now, I may not know all that much about guitars and all of that, but don't they have metal in them? And if you couldn't handle metal, being around metal all the time as it is in your room, would be kind of bad, correct? Yet, Mackie has no problem with the metal in his room like that, but he can't handle any metal.

And he can touch the lockers no problem at all, which, I believe, are made of metal. Again, I could be wrong. I've only played the piano so I don't know about guitars and maybe lockers aren't made of metal, but still...

Anyways, the entire time Mackie is just this dull, lifeless, boring guy I don't even know what he looks like or sounds like or anything about him. He's just like a lego brick or Bella Swan. I can't even determine if he's male or not because he doesn't sound like a male. He sounds like a whinny teenage girl.

However, he'll talk about how everyone is fake in town and they aren't real. And when Tate comes to him (and stalks him) and says how everyone is fake and she's sick of it, he tells her to stop bothering him, to shut up, to ignore it. Um... what? So, you have no problem complaining to yourself about how everything is fake, but when someone else actually has the same idea as you, you freak out and have to be all normal and fake?

And why in the world is Tate stalking him? She randomly goes up to Mackie and says she knows about him or something and decides to attach herself to him by stalking him and saying he must listen to her because she knows something isn't right. Why? Oh, yeah, just a random plot point that makes no sense.

What's also ironic to note is that Mackie has been trying to blend in with everyone and be invisible, so to speak, yet his eyes are completely black and they stand out like crazy. He's also allergic to metal and can't stand blood. Yet, no one notices these things and doesn't ask any questions because "he's normal".

I don't know about you, but I'm getting a little skeptical about the entire thing. It's just one big pile of crap that doesn't make sense. But I go on, thinking it'll all make sense.

It turns out Mackie is basically dying and needs some drug to help him live. He manages to get this drug, but it's with a cost. He has to stay with these... whatever they are things and play in a band. A rock band. If you're like me, you're probably wondering why in the world would people who are allergic to metal be in a rock band and what the point of this has to do with anything. My guess is that Brenna just wanted to be in a rock band in her life but never had a chance to so she used writing as an escape for that. Because that's all I can think of why it would be important for creatures who can die from metal to be so close to metal all the time.

What's worse is that she explains why. -_-

These creatures play in a rock band to make the people in town happy so that every seven years, these creatures can steal a child and sacrifice it and make the town prosper and the entire time these creatures gain some sort of power from this happiness. Sad thing is, the town is a piece of crap that hasn't prospered in a long time. Still wondering why they would be willing to sacrifice their children to these creatures. And being in a rock band as well. I don't mind rock on occasion, but listening to a rock band all the time is not going to make me happy whatsoever. I guess people in this depressing town don't mind that.

Really, by now, the story is filled with so many plot holes it's not even funny. And it's halfway through the book with no real explanation or plot so far. This is also about the time when I decide I can't take anymore and stop reading it. I did take a peak at the end to see if it would be worth reading all the way through and it wasn't. It really wasn't.

I have no idea what these creature things look like or are. At first I thought they were faeries because that's what it was presented to me. But no where in the book does it say that. They don't even sound like any other creatures either. More like a mix of vampires, demons and faeries or something. I don't get it.

If the metal is so deadly to them, WHY DO THEY KEEP TOUCHING IT?! Why do they spend so much time around it?! WHY?

If the town is a piece of crap, why does the town do NOTHING to stop these things?

Why are the parents okay with a replacement taking their child, knowing their real child is dead and they have this thing that's just freaky?

Why do these creatures replace their children with human children if they're going to die as soon as they switch them? They willingly know the child is going to die, yet they switch them in hopes they have a better future.

Why is it "love" makes these creatures beautiful when no one would actually love this creature that is a replacement for their child? I mean, you have a child and it's taken from you and killed and all you have is some creature that looks like your child but isn't and you have to love it?

Why is it these creatures look just like the children they take?

Why is nothing consistent? It's described as strange and normal. They can't handle loud noises, but they're in a rock band. They recognize each other and don't recognize each other. They feel relieved and worse than that. Um... what? Stop contradicting yourself! Make up your stupid mind and pick one or the other. You can't have both and make it work. It doesn't work.

And then we have the writing. The simple, stupid, basic writing that any beginner writer knows not to do. The "I did this. Then this happened. Then I did this. I felt this." Over and over. I have critiqued others who have written pieces of crap like that telling them that's not a good way to write. It's dull, boring, and it tells everything and shows nothing. You want to SHOW your story, NOT tell it. Brenna just tells her boring stupid story and we're supposed to accept it? I don't think so.

There was no depth in the characters. Everyone was a flat cardboard cutout. Boring and dull. That's what happens when you don't build your character.

And the conversations... I don't know anyone who says half the things in this book. "Socially lubricated"? What? What does that even mean?!

What makes all of this worse is the fact that Brenna actually was an editor BEFORE she started writing and decided to write instead. No lie, she says she was an editor beforehand. After I read that, I'm almost thankful she's not editing anymore. With crappy writing like what she has, who would want her as an editor? People who don't get paid to edit can edit a lot better than she can write.

It's not just that the story is filled with plot holes, but also that it's just written bad as well makes me hate this story and refuse to recommend it. I can't even finish reading it. It doesn't make any sense and has contradictions like crazy. Thank you, but I think I'll have people actually understand what's going on.
rows.title: Contradictions and plot holes

rows.text:
I JUST purchased the 'NEW FOR 2016, 10 lb capacity model #PAN310DG'.
I will truly try to keep this short as I would like to save others from my mistake.

Okay, I took a chance due to all of the reviews (most of which did not apply to my model but still I took a chance).
I did my research in 1 day (yep, not much time).... but unless you rec'd what I did and furthered your search ----- Well, you would NEVER had learned what I did!
Additionally, I will be including only a limited number of photos here (in hopes that (as usual, photos get top priority in the review) as I really would rather not waste any more of my time on something that I will be returning the same day it arrived.)
Think what you may, but this item really has issues! And I can almost promise that no one wants to deal with them.
And I reiterate, ONLY DUE to the number of reviews did I purchase this VS The Laundry Alternative 'anything else' !!

One other item, regarding the pics:
One is how I ended up having to feed water into the unit AND the carpet samples to keep it from creeping.... the other is the FURTHERED water that the 'drainage hose' accumulated whilst writing this review (hopefully you can see the 'sort of blue water in the hose' ---- WHEN IT HAD ALREADY DRAINED COMPLETELY THE FIRST TIME!!! Yes, it would further accumulate due to no 'spinning dry effect' but do you really want to deal with a potential mess tomorrow morning or whenever!?!?!!!!!

PROS:
It TRULY IS A NICE BIG washing tub!!! (best I can do here....sorry Panda)
Beyond that, nothing else regarding this machine impresses me NOR is worth my (or your) time&effort.

CONS:
Oh my, where to start?!?
I rent, so of course I am worried about 'leakage' from anywhere.
Once it arrived, I cancelled the rest of my day to test this out.
1) Instructions are from a foreign country with attempted English. I'm okay with that, but really? Thankfully I had read enough of the reviews and instructions to make it work.

2) The water input and output hoses are a joke!
My water input hose had leaks in 2 places causing water to disperse everwhere when attempting to use it. They are SOOO VERY THIN, not to mention that the input water hose wouldn't even 'attach' to a newer Kohler faucet properly. Seeing that, I avoided the intake hose and just basically used the 'green bowl that you will see in the pic' in order to fill it up initially. I wanted to run this without ANY CLOTHES inside in order to see if it leaked anywhere.

3) There is a reason that the instructions say to 'ADD soap and agitate for ONLY 1-2 minutes'. While I added no soap, with water filled ONLY 1/4 of the way ---- the entire unit started CREEPING ALONG on my bathroom counter ALMOST to the end of it once I set the timer. Thankfully, I caught that moment in time before a disaster happened. Vibration & CREEPING is huge on this item with only water!
a) With only water inside the tub and the timer set to approx 10 min, drips of water
began leaking from the 'top lift-up cover' down the machine and under the unit & onto the counter.
Thankfully, I was there to see this and caught the issue before something may have
caused an electrical issue due to the motor being on the bottom of the unit. At that point,
I learned that yes, only 1-2 min of agitation would be best for laundry soap added.
b) Additonally, I still needed to use some vinyl coated carpet samples that I had and hoped that
might alleviate the CREEPING. They seemed to help.

4) I decided that with perhaps some weight of clothes and detergent AND YES it did....
However, I did not take 15 minutes to watch to see if either the carpet samples or the machine continued to 'creep' towards the edge side of my counter. At this point, I was 'SO completely 'uninthralled' over the entire thing' and switched off the timer. I DO NOT HAVE TIME TO 'literally watch' my washing machine!!! Do you?? So, that's when I went to google and youtube and found 'what might HOPEFULLY make me happier'. On youtube, watch 'thomfoz' for Almost Everything The Laundry Alternative! While I had read about the 3 year warr from 'TLA' ---- the lack of reviews here on amazon prevented my initial instincts to purchase one of their 'portable/countertop washing machines'.

5) One more 'con'...... you'd BETTER HAVE A NICE BIG SINK (or tub) to allow the drainage tube to drain into!
After all of the above efforts, I decided to wash a couple tshirts and potholders w/soap. One of the tshirts bled some blue and for the first time ---- I could see that the UPWARD HOOKED-ON DRAINAGE TUBE (it works on gravity to drain, but) was at or more than 3/4 filled with water! I get it --- upward facing and gravity ------but did I really want to WORRY about this tube spilling over!?!?!??? ......AND this is why I am taking the time to write this review!!! I was BEYOND DONE with every BAD issue this machine had!!

I'm sure there were more things that were wrong regarding the Panda unit.... but I've no time left to 'even take the time here'....... it really just sorta sucks!!! The one (other than warr) thing that TLA offers beyond a 3 year warr, is that their SuperPoP has a PUMP to 'UPSUCK' the drainage water into a kitchen sink!!! It comes with a hook which or which may not work with your countertop ----- but NO MORE (issue #5 abv) hoping that you don't spill all over the counter and floor the drainage water!
NO GRAVITY DRAINAGE INVOLVED!!!!
AND, while the hook may not work on your countertop, you sure will not be attempting to control the flow of the drainage water (while you attempt to place it into your sink without spilling!!!!!!!!!) You can just lay it into your sink if need be!!!
This SURE BEATS attempting to keep a lot of water from flowing all over the counter depending on the angle that you are holding the drainage tube BEFORE EVEN MAKING IT STEADY ENUF TO FLOW INTO YOUR SINK ONLY!!!! There is absolutely no CONTROL of the drainage tube on the Panda....do your best of grab a roll of paper towels!!
Beyond that, the SuperPop HAS A RINSE CYCLE that ONLY ALLOWS WATER OUT !!!
Should you EVER UNHOOK the upward facing drainage tube on the Panda downwards (or the hook breaks)....... you'll be left with a mess of water everywhere ---- let alone a poss lawsuit from your downstairs neighbor for all of the damage. Whether it be your home or the tenant below ---- do you really want to deal with the mess that this might cause?!???

My unit is going back defective ----- 2 holes in the intake hose.....
My next attempt will be 'something, anything!' from TLA!!!
With a 3 year warr, and a pump to PUSH WATER UPWARDS into the sink ---- I would rather have that fail than this crappy thing! This Panda unit causes too much concern, too much mess, and too much wasted time otherwise!!
I hope this might help others find the 'right purchase' their first time.
PS: have not purchased TLA Superpop yet, but after this review posts ---- I WILL BE!!
I just don't see how it can be as much as an issue as this unit. Esp, because it has that pump to extract water upwards into a sink VS a loose canon of a hose that pressurizes water and CANNOT WAIT TO SPOUT it's contents out!!

Again.... I REITERATE!!!! ------The Laundry Alternative has a 3 year warranty on ALL OF THEIR PRODUCTS. The Panda ONLY has a 6 month warranty. See 'thomfoz' on youtube for other options to your needs!!! I am hoping that his 'love of reviewing washing machines' meets my final choice needs and perhaps yours instead of this product :)

PPS: I had taken the chance after all of my research to purchase the TLA Nina Spin Dryer..... it just arrived. While I do not know about the TLA SuperPop ---- the Nina Spin Dryer has HUGE RUBBER FEET (almost suction cup like). I will be testing this tonight to wring out my still soapy Panda Wash (my bad, too much detergent apparently)...... and I don't know if the SuperPop will include the same rubber feet.... but I am thrilled with the TLA Nina Spin Dryer already!!! Something to prevent creeeping along any floors or countertops!!!!! Hoping it does just that and that the SuperPop comes with the same xlnt rubber feet once it arrives!!!
Good luck to you all.... I DO truly hope this helps your purchase happiness!!!

(PPPS: I try to avoid reviews due to the cost going up when folks love what they get.... but I just couldn't NOT review this Panda and sleep well tonight :)
rows.title: Live & Learn the BAD way....or RETURN this for The Laundry Alternative SuperPop (even cheaper)...

rows.text:
First my background. I am an IT consultant responsible for deploying servers, workstations, and mobile devices. I have/had personally owned a tablet device that has 1 of the 3 major Mobile OSs. I had a ASUS Transformer for 30 days and liked it, I add the dock and like it...but didnt love it. I have a number of iOS device Touch, Pad, Phone. And now the Webos powered TouchPad (TP). I like them all in different ways but the touchpad seems to be what I use most. maybe because it is the newest...but there is something about it that I like. It seems as if Apple and Google had a child this would be it. It has many of their strengths but not many of there weaknesses...it has its own weaknesses but much because it is new. So lets start and look at these devices to see what makes them different and which one stands on in its respective category. This is also a post that is going on my blog for it has taken me a long time to test and review these product before I could write this.

First...keyboard...a way to interface with a device is critical. Touchscreens have all became pretty similar in regards to pinch to zoom, scrolling, selecting, etc. But the keyboard is something that has been why I think so many are scared to get into tablets. Sure they lack that tactile feel but some do a better job then others. So with that said the onscreen keyboard on the touchpad is my favoirte of all 3. For many reason, first is onscreen keyboard more so the users make far more mistakes. So have a spell checker is a must...android/honeycomb...they skipped out on this...big mistake. Not to mention visually the keyboard is unappealing. It makes tons of mistakes and seem the least forgiving. Apple...very good the original, inline spell checker, responsive, polished but after using the TP it is in second place. The Touchpad keyboard has the number keys above...something the iOS does not. The TP keyboard is comfortable the sounds it make are not intrusive and just plan works...the 3D key look is nice not to mention the transitional. So in the interface department the TP seems to be well thought out and engineered.

Multi-tasking on the webOS tablet...touchpad...is light years ahead, iOS is plain horrible and is not a true multitasking system it uses a handful of "services" mostly notification to make it feel as if it isnt multi-tasking. Double pumping the middle button make the multi-tasking menu of running apps appear. To close them is terrible and not well thought out. Apple needs to go back to the drawing board!!! Android/Honeycomb is good and much better than iOS but it is still unorganized and completely unpolished! Android gets sooo choppy when memory usage approaches its limits. Honeycomb is much better then froyo but still not that great in comparison to the webos TP. The card system is just better...its intuitive, simple, functional, smarter just plain easier to switch between and flat out know what is running! It is as I have already said light years ahead!

Account integration is far better on the TP. The others seem to require more apps to get more accounts up on the device. Out of the box the TP has tons of account integration. I dont need "An app for that" I use one app for email and grab 6 of my accounts...android/honeycomb...ah no. I check, Hotmail, two Gmail accounts, and a Yahoo account on it. I use one email app on the TP...its native. And set it up on in a combined inbox. iPad...yes it work very similar like the TP but the function is a little nicer in my opinion on the TP. Android is just a mess..I need the gmail app for my gmail, the yahoo app for my yahoo (Froyo...honeycomb now does a little better job on this) and the built in email app for hotmail. Sloppy! But the email app on honey is quite nice looking and replying back I like a little better on it...but once again the lack of spell checking is just stupid on behalf of google! But before I stray off. Account integration also steams throughout the entire webos...they call it synergy. What ever they call it I like it! When I setup my 2 gmail account it automatically added them to my Message app on the TP. Not to mention I added a skype account to the messaging app as well. Oh and did I mention calendar...it does those as well. It also integrate throughout webos, Facebook, LinkedIn, Photobucket, Box.net, Dropbox, yahoo, aol, the list goes on and on! Android and apple dont even come close! But that still isnt to say the TP is missing. Dropbox is limited and not every aspect of these account is fully put into the TP...but it is still more then the others!

IMing...TP kills all!! I need multiply 3rd party apps on iOS and Android. And 3rd party free apps have ads!! Oh and most 3rd party free apps I hate to say it lack polish! My one messaging app on TP does, skype, yahoo, google, and AIM!!! And it does them well!! The interface works isnt garbage up and runs as it should. eBuddy...not even close! I have tried every IMing app for both iOS and Android and they just arent close!

Web-browsing. TP once again kills it. My transformer even after the update force closes!! I do like the tabs in Android...really nice but they get choppy and hard to navigate if you have too many open...becomes a pig! And Apple...dont get me started...slow and plugin missing black screens...Apple really dropped the ball here. But then again you can ususally get an app for many of the sites that dont work...but at what cost and for the love of god how many apps do I really want and need!!! it gets a little stupid after a while. The apps are nice for they are engineered and optimized for a tablet experience. Some more of many lack. But they help bring the web experience missing back to the iPad...but it doesnt bring everything back! Many of the major sites are re-written to work with the iPad. My company and many other cant afford to re-write our sites to HTML 5 to support the iPad...it is expensive and apple should have done a better job. Sure there is Skyfire but it is nowhere near that good! The Touchpad is as close to a desktop browser as a tablet can come! Flash isnt everything but it still makes up over 50% of the website on the internet!! My company site included! Bookmarks are nice on the TP but I do wish it had tabs. Everytime you want a new site to open you need a new tab, unless you are just working from the original...not really a problem but I tend to have several tabs open on my desktop, usually 15-25 at a time. The tablet I dont typically do this.

Notification system. In this order TP, Android, Apple. Apple is just plan terrible...intrusive, annoying and just old...iOS5 is changing this...but that is yet to be released. As of now TP notification system is organized, intuitive, elegent, visual and most important doesnt get in the way. It works at the lock screen as well! Android is decent but still sloppy when compared to webos. I like how clean the TP system works. My iProducts drive me nuts with the current notification system...email...pops up right over what you are doing...click "view" or click "close"...view takes you away from what you are doing. "Close" gone into thin air! it plain sucks! Android as I said is better then apple but cluttered again and disorganized! I still would take it over apple but it needs some work.

Apps...TP is dead last...much because it is 6 weeks old compared to the others. But more of a concern is since HP is giving the device side a dirt nap what happens to the software side. webos was never given enough time to interest developers now this is even worse considering the current state of webos. A big black eye. However since the web browsing isnt so crippled I can do a ton without the need for many apps...just like I do on my Mac or My Win7 box...I dont need a ton of apps on it. 90% of what I do is via a browser! Apple is an absolute app dream...there are over 3000 weather apps...many garbage but definitely a few more then impressive ones! There apps are typically more polished feature rich and tablet or phone optimized. I love pulse!! There are sooo many but sometimes navigating and remember which ones I have is a pain! Honeycomb...well not much better then the TP. Although I do have pulse, new360, teamviewer. Apple is light years ahead...the quality is excellent and these apps do more and increase the capability of the iProducts so beyond that any have imagined! But then again I give the iPad a 4 1/2 overall as well...the app part is a 5 all day long!!! But remember this...apple apps usually cost money...newspaper, magazines...etc. Some stuff you can usually get online free. But as I said it is stll more then that.

Hardware...HP is dead last. But not horrible. Rear camera is missing...never used mine and my transfomer, not sure if because it sucks or I just dont carry my tablet around to take pictures. I do use the front for skype and googletalk. No expansion...once again I never found a need for it, not on apple of tp. But the android tabs have it. The android tablets I believe run away with this because there are so many different version of it. For example the ASUS has a dock...that also has a battery in it. Keyboard is nice and it even has a mouse. So it feel like e netbook. The docks keyboard is stellar! Not to mention optimized for honeycomb with hotkeys. Battery life was excellent on the Ipad and the Asus. As for TP, good but not as good. The more cycles the better it got. It is about an hour to 90 mins less then the other. The asus with the dock is ridiculous...tons of time. Processor on the TP is slower and feels a little slower but not horrible and doesnt seem to feel like it is getting slower like the asus did when too many apps where loaded. The Apple screen is amazing but then again so is the asus...in fact the asus screen I like more! TP last in screen, it isnt as bright but cant say I have ran into anytimes that I thought it wasnt bright enough. Speakers..the TP kicks the crap out of both...no match! It does lack expansion ports but so does apple and they seem to be fine without it. I am yet to use the ports on the asus. But I do like the touchstone dock...quick easy and works great! And I also like the fact that the charging cable is not exclusive to the device...couch couch apple and asus. I can use any microUSB cable to charge or plug in the TP. I have used my samsung charger to charge my TP...yes it works fine! I do not like the glossy finger print magnet shell on the TP. But I put the HP rubberized case with easel on it and never looked back. Yes it is heavy and thicker but it is nominal at best. These tablets are so light and I am not 90 years old. This not even worth a talking point.

Now a few areas of pluses and minus for the touchpad in comparison. For starters the backup function is awesome!!! Right to the cloud for your apps, settings, contact, cal, etc. No movies, music, or phones...which would have been cool but replacement or coming off a reset is very quick to restore. Android...pass...it is start over city, yeah there are a few apps to back it up the the SD card but it is technical and I have seen android eat SD Card...especially rooted devices! Apple...with itunes (Which is a love hate handcuffing situation) is nice from a restore standpoint. Easy to restore to a new device or restore from a reset device. And it resets everything! But you need to do it all via itunes. This is changing. The copy, paste, highlight absolutely sucks on the TP!!!! I hate it...the others are killing it here. I wish it had widgets..I am not missing them that much on the TP but some I did like on android. Apple...none. The calendar is sluggish on the TP and I really like the android and apple ones better. I only use one calendar so I dont see the benefits as well as other might with the TP. For the TP handles multi-cals pretty good. No push for Hotmail kinda sucks but I can wait 5 minutes. Just type is pretty nice. It scours the entire device and can also scour sites if you like. I cannot do this on the others. It is a nice way to weed thru the crap faster.

All in all for a now defunct product that can be had for 99 or 149 this is a fantastic tablet. I ripped some movies via handbrake, copied my music, check my email, surf, and IM this is a stellar device. If you need certain apps this is not for you. I dont miss them and I find myself using this more then the other, could be because it is new but I like the web on it better. I like email on it better. I actually like music and movies on it better as well. Oh and the facebook app is soo much nicer then the others, No ads and it is polished! For the core functions it has in common with the others. It is a better device in many ways. The interface doesnt feel fisher price like my ipad does and iphone. It is clean, organized, intuitive and smooth. Too bad it is leaving the party early for I think it had a future. HP just blew something in the hardware that might have made it a stand out.
rows.title: Head to toe review and comparison!  I like it

rows.text:
This is a kind of a two part review since I can't give the book a specific overall rating really because it kind of has two halves and each half has radically different ratings. The first half { pre where Leroux picked up for his book } I would give a 4 to a 4.5 and the part where Leroux picks up gets a resounding 0 as much as I hate to have to review it that low.

Disclaimer: I did not read this book from a specifically Leroux purist point of view { mostly because I'd already heard that I shouldn't do it that way or I would be disappointed } but I will point out the areas where the Leroux purist in me wasn't too crazy about how the book was being written.

This is a two part review kind of since I can't give the book a specific overall rating really because it kind of has two halves and each half has radically different ratings. The first half { pre where Leroux picked up for his book } I would give a 4 to a 4.5 and the part where Leroux picks up gets a resounding 0 as much as I hate to have to review it that low.

Disclaimer: I did not read this book from a specifically Leroux purist point of view { mostly because I'd already heard that I shouldn't do it that way or I would be disappointed } but I will point out the areas where the Leroux purist in me wasn't too crazy about how the book was being written.

Here's my review on the first part:

Three points to Susan Kay for the way she portrayed Erik's mother. While I can't say the way she portrayed his mother was very Leroux accurate she did manage to portray his mother in a rather thought provoking light. The way she portrayed his mother she came across to me as the kind of mother who was fighting the war between her motherly instinct to properly take care of her son and the side that worried what others thought about her. It seemed like as soon as I actually started thinking his mother might not be half bad she once again did something so incredibly cruel I wanted to throw something at her and yell "but you were getting so much better!!!!!" It was an interesting portrayal to say the least and while I'm still not entirely crazy about his mom the one in the Kay novel isn't quite as bad as the one in the Leroux novel who practically refused to ever see him.

You'll have to read the book to see why I can't do much of a review on his father.

Marie { Erik's mother's more level headed friend } I frigging loved! SPOILER ALERT!!!!! The moment she smacks Erik's mother and flips out on her was absolutely priceless. Bahahaha!
END OF SPOILER ALERT!!!!!

Sasha { the family dog } I loved as well. :XD: She { I'm assuming it's a she } was awesome. Any friend of Erik's is pure awesome.

The other characters in the first "chapter" of the book { the section where Erik's mother is narrarating } I'm kind of middle of the road on. I liked one or two of them at the beginning but then they'd go crazy and I don't have the time at the moment to read over that section again quick and tell you who all was good and who wasn't.

On to the next "chapter".

Since Erik is my favorite character during the entirety of the first half of the book pre Leroux and he's the only one in this section who I really liked { everybody else needs slapped and the guy who locked him up in the cage we ain't even touching but you'll read about that in the book } then this is all I really have for review for this "chapter" which wasn't that long anyway. Sorry about that.

Next "chapter"

I liked Giovanni { the guy who teaches Erik stone masonry :D }. He was pretty awesome.

Luciana { is one big humongous spoiler alert so to those of you who haven't read the book you can skip down to the bold print below that signifies the end of the spoiler }
I liked Luciana more than I liked Christine in this book and if it weren't for how this chapter ended I would totally be Team Erik/Luciana. :/

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
END OF SPOILER ALERT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
The ending of this "chapter" made me really sad. Not because it was terribly written but because it was a sad ending. Read it yourself and you'll see why. { You'll notice I don't put many spoilers in my review to make it even harder for those who haven't read the book to try to get an opinion on the book based on my opinion instead of reading it themselves. }

Next "chapter" { where we meet the Daroga who in this book is called Nadir }

This entire chapter is pretty awesome except for a few nitpicks here and there.

Daroga is, as usual, pure awesome epic win sauce. { Duh! lol :D }

Reza { Daroga otherwise known as Nadir has a son in this book :D } is such a sweetie. Their { Erik and Reza's } moments together for the most part were absolutely adorable and I swear my brain almost exploded from the cuteness. *squees*

I did not like having the little sultana from the Leroux novel get replaced by the Shah's mother { or Sultan's mother I forget which since there were so many higher ups in that chapter }. Even not looking at it from a Leroux purist stand point it still didn't work for me. Sorry but it just didn't.

Next "chapter"

We're back to Erik again and transitioning from pre Leroux to where Leroux picked up in Erik's life. This is the point in the book where the awesomeness flattens out into okie dokieness and then starts its rapid downward spiral right near the end. I pretty well liked this chapter except for a few nitpicks here and there prolly until it came to the fateful scene where Erik sees Christine for the first time and my high opinion of the book crashes to the floor. As a prequel to the Leroux novel it's pretty stinking awesome but once she continues on to the point where Leroux picked up in his novel that's when I get disgusted. Although I have to admit Ayesha was a nice addition in this chapter. :)

Second half of the book where the awesomeness comes crashing down:

I had already heard this book wasn't completely Leroux accurate and had seen plenty of reviews from phans/fans who loved the first part of the book but hated the second part. It's safe to say that after having completely read this book I am now officially a part of that camp that prefers the first part and majorly dislikes the second part. This entire part of the review from here on out is one big humongous spoiler alert that I'm putting in here to prepare those who are major Leroux purists for the potential heart attack they may have after reading the second part of this book and also because I have some nit picks of my own that aren't specifically coming from a Leroux purist point of view.

First off I'm sorry but there was absolutely no real true chemistry between Erik and Christine. To me it felt more like they were majorly sexually attracted to each other and that was it { Susan Kay is very obviously Team Erik and Christine by this point in the book even though Christine does still end up with Raoul at the end of the book }. Honestly I liked Erik and Luciana's relationship waaaaay better then I liked the relationship between him and Christine and if the end of the "chapter" that involved Luciana had gone differently who knows but they might have ended up together and Christine would never have taken part in Erik's life the way she does in this book.
Also I still have no idea whether Erik completely sold his soul to the devil near the end of this part or not but he kept getting really close during this half of the book and that pissed me off that Kay thought he would do that.
Poor Don Juan Triumphant gets turned into the masturbatory song of the century *vomits all over the floor at this monstrosity of a destruction of that song* when Erik plays it of his own accord without any unmasking or anything in the next room while Christine gets it on with a pillow in her room { and yes Kay does get a bit descriptive as to what exactly is going on at that point -__- }.
The unmasking was crap. This is pretty much my summary of Kay's unmasking: Christine unmasks Erik. Erik goes "oh crap she unmasked me. Now what?" { yeah he's pretty freaking calm about it especially compared to the Leroux and even earlier unmaskings in the book from other people } then proceeds to have a seizure { because we all know he couldn't have been groveling at her feet for any other reason then that he was having a seizure } and then not long after that everythings all honky dory happy puppies and pretty unicorns land. Really? Really?! REALLY?!?!?!?!?!
Also no matter how much I wish Erik could have "gotten the girl" at the end of the book { or at least have lived long enough to move on and get another girl } Christine was NOT madly in love with Erik no matter what anyone says. I'm sorry but that's just how it is. { Just in case you were wondering Christine and I "don't get along well." }
Also Kay's pulls a Dance!Erik by having Christine look like Erik's mother. I'm not entirely crazy about that concept even in the Yeston & Kopit version of POTO and in this one it gets really really disgusting when I think how little Erik and Christine are attracted to each other personality wise in this version.
This is the part that makes my skin crawl the most when I think about how a later novel { think Phantom of Manhattan -__- } did this as well: Christine has Erik's kid { even better is how she gets it on with him pretty much the night before her wedding if I'm understanding things correctly which sounds incredible Love Never Dies now doesn't it? } and yes Christine does also die { quite a few years after Erik but still and she dies of cancer instead of a bullet a la Phantom of Manhattan and Love Never Dies but still Susan Kay tried to pull the whole "together in eternity" thing there when she had Christine die off from cancer years later.
I have to give credit where credits is due though that she did manage to not emasculate Raoul's character { whether it be figuratively a la Love Never Dies or figuratively and literally a la Phantom of Manhattan }.
Also three points to Susan Kay for making it no denying that Buquet hung himself in the torture chamber since there are those out there who say Erik killed him in the Leroux novel and I don't quite agree with that { though he really does in the Andrew Lloyd Webber version } so it was nice to see that in this novel.
I have other nitpicks as well but it's late and I don't have the time to list every last little nit pick.

P.S.
In the acknowledgements section at the end of the book Susan Kay mentions a 1967 cartoon version of Phantom of the Opera. That is a typo. It's actually 1987 just in case anyone wanted to go see it. It's easily found on youtube just by searching Phantom of the Opera cartoon.
rows.title: A Review Dividing The Book Into Two Halves

rows.text:
Hey Everyone, who's looking for an alternate ending?? Well check out Stephanie Ziel's alternate ending @ Addicted Readers: [...]

Its was AMAZING!!! I wish Veronica Roth would of written an ending like that!!

***UPDATED***

***REVIEW****

ALLEGIANT, Oh, ALLEGIANT, how you failed me in so many ways!

I went over every feeling, every thought, every emotion I had, be it good or bad. And after all that analyzing in my OWN opinion, I still came back with NOT being satisfied one bit with this Conclusion to my favorite series, or should I say "Used To Be My Favorite Series"?

FIRST LET ME TALK ABOUT MY FEELINGS ON THE ENDING:

WARNING A BUNCH OF SPOILERS:

***READ AT YOUR OWN RISK***
|
|
|
|
|
|
|
V
I had this book spoiled for me before I started reading ALLEGIANT, so I went into it already knowing what was going to happen to Tris in the ending. I found out about it 2 days before I started reading, and I was an emotional wreck! My husband thought I was insane! But I was stunned, I really could not believe Tris was going to die. I had no idea, not even a little thought that it would happen to Tris. So when I did find out, I was shocked! I was in denial for the first couple hours until finally I couldn't take it anymore and I broke down and read some of the reviews! Then, I was FORCED to see the truth, Tris would in fact die!! UGH, I still get teary eyed when I think about it! I never would of thought Veronica would do that to Tris, and not just to Tris, but to her LOYAL FANS! Tris had so many near death experiences, especially in INSURGENT, when she battled with death, and wanting to die, but finally she had an eye opener and realized she did not want to die, and that she was more then a self sacficer. So that led me to believe Tris had all the near death experiences that she was going to have, boy was I wrong!!!

I stand 100% behind MY Opinion, that this WAS THE WRONG WAY TO END THE DIVERGENT SERIES!!!

AGAIN, MY OPINION!!!

Veronica said that she had this ending planed from the very beginning of this series, and that it was the ONLY ending that she felt would do this series any justice! WHAT, NO, WRONG VERONICA! She said she felt having these characters end with a happy ending, holding hands, walking off into the sunset was fake and unbelievable. BUT wait, I came across an alternate ending to ALLEGIANT written by a Fan Fiction author Stephanie Ziel (POST HERE), and let me tell you, IT WAS AMAZING!!! And, NO, they did not have this sugarcoated happy ending like Veronica didn't want! They had a hard ending, that brought tears to my eyes, but still TRIS SURRIVED!!! And IN MY OPINION it was the BEST ENDING FOR THIS SERIES!!! If Veronica would of wrote this ending I think A LOT more people would of been more satisfied, including me!!

Now, all that said, I was still very upset that Tris died. I thought it was the wrong move for Veronica to make. (I am NOT the only one who thought this, as of the date of this review, Amazon's has a 2.8 Rating, Not good, not good at all). BUT I wasn't just upset that Tris died, I didn't like the WAY Tris died! I thought Tris sacrificing herself and taking her brothers place in the Weapons Lab, was the worst way for Tris to go! I mean I get it, this book is about selflessness and forgiving, and all that blah, blah, blah, BUT if Veronica Roth was going to kill off the main protagonist she should of at least had her go out with a bang, in which she DEFINITELY did not!

THESE ARE THE MANY OTHER REASONS I DID NOT LIKE ALLEGIANT:

Okay, the ending was not my only problem with ALLEGIANT. My first issue was the alternate POV's. I HATED IT!! It irritated me to no end! UGH, it drove me insane!! I could not tell Tobias and Tris apart to save my life. Only way I was able to tell them apart was when I went back to the beginning chapter and read the heading of who's POV it was. If not for the heading with the name of who's chapter it was, I would of been lost and waiting until whoever POV it was, said the other persons name. But their voice was so similar, that at times I just wanted to put the book down and scream, yes it was that bad for me!!

Another issue I had was Tobias! He was a totally different character from the first 2 books. I mean a different guy all together! ONE of the MANY reasons I LOVED Four was that he always had this sexy mysterious way about him, that just kept me wanting to know him more. And when I read he's POV in the Short Stories, I was in awe! I loved the tough, strong-willed, determined, guarded Four. And being in his mind was really such a treat! He had all these character traits that I loved so much in the Short Stories. So I was expecting to have the same in ALLEGIANT, but of course with more emotion because in the short stories he wasn't with Tris or in love. So I was curious to see how Four would react to being in love and having someone else to worry about besides himself. BUT WOW, what I got was A BIG FAT DISAPPOINTMENT!!!

At times Four acted naïve, like he was new too their world and all the plotting, scheming, betrayals, and death around him. It caused him many problems that he couldn't get out of! UGH, and he was WAY to trusting with the first pretty girl that feed him a load of crap! Since when did Tobias become so trusting with complete strangers that he was willing to go against everything he believed in? Not the Tobias I have grown to love and care deeply for!

Another thing that changed in ALLEGIANT with the character traits, was that Tris became the strong, sure of herself girl, and Four was the weak, trusting and unsure of who and where he belonged guy. And that was not the Four I've come to love! Nope, this new and NOT improved Four in ALLEGIANT --SPOILER-- was so trusting, that it even cost Uriah his life!! I'm not saying it was directly Four's fault, because it wasn't. But if he would of trusted Tris and her judgment, and not trusted Nita, and went along with her insane plan, then the whole mission would of had to wait, then Uriah wouldn't of been standing where he was when it all happened! So again, if Four would of went with his heart instead of his quick impulse, then certain things wouldn't of happened. Which honestly, I felt some thing's happened with really no reasoning behind it. Like Uriah, what was the whole purpose of killing him off??? Really I would love to know, because I can't figure it out. Again, he was another one of my favorite characters that got killed off, FOR WHAT???? Four was just so annoying, and most of the time I couldn't' wait to get back to Tris's POV. But even Tris had her annoying chapters too! I felt the total opposite about all of this in the first 2 books! I LOVED pretty much everything about DIVERGENT & INSURGENT! But ALLLEGIANT just fell flat for me in SO MANY WAYS, so more reasons then what I'm putting in this review!!! If I listed all the reasons why I hated ALLEGIANT, then we would be here all day, because their is SO many! Seriously!

Then the plot was so annoying, the whole what was outside of the fence was stupid, and definitely not what I was expecting! (I say that NOT in a good way) The GD's and GP's, was the worst idea Veronica has ever came up with! I was expecting something so unique and original from Veronica, because that's all she's ever given me! I kept waiting for the big BANG to happen when they went behind the boundaries of their city. I was hoping for a drawn out scary reason for them being horded off and locked away like dogs! But the whole Gene thing was just laughable! When I first found out the reasoning behind everything, I was just staring at the book, saying "What Did I Just Read? This Can Not Be It? Their HAS TO BE MORE"? I kept waiting for it all to be a big hoax's, but it wasn't, and I was let down for the hundredth time with this book! I just couldn't take it anymore. Then by the time I got to the ending, I just didn't have it in me to be mad, or upset, or cry anymore! I've had enough letdowns to last me a bookish lifetime just in this one book! And its SO HEARTBREAKING to feel this way about my favorite series!!!

I honestly can not think of one thing that I liked about this book! As of the beginning of this review I was planning on giving this book 2 stars, but now that I voiced some, NOT even ALL of my issues on paper, I realized that their really wasn't anything I enjoyed in this last book! So I feel obligated to give this book 1 star! And oh, how that makes me feel terrible! I really, really, really, wanted to love this book! I did, I really did, but I just can't for all the reasons stated above, and more not stated! I can't stress enough how much I LOVED the first 2 books, DIVERGENT & INSURGENT, and I had such high hopes for ALLEGIANT, maybe too high, because I was let down HARD!! I just can't love this book, or even like it, and I can't give it more then a 1 star rating! And I've never given a 1 star rating, NEVER! At least until this book! SO it is really hard for me to give it this 1 star, but it really does deserve it, in MY OPINION!

ON A FINAL NOTE:

So, I will walk away from this series heartbroken, torn,
lost, and unsure if I should ever put as much hope, faith and trust in another author the way I put forth in Veronica Roth! Will I read more of her books? I really don't know! She is such a skilled writer, that just took a big dive down in ALLEGIANT, but that does NOT make her any less of an author in my opinion. So I would hope Veronica would make ALLEGIANT a learning experience and learn from the many mistakes she made in this last book, and not make them again. So as of right now, who knows, depends on if I will ever feel inclined to pick up a book with Veronica Roth's name on the cover!

So there you have it, what I hated the most about ALLEGIANT! What are you thoughts on ALLEGIANT?? Did you love it, hate it, didn't really care either way?? But for all of you who feel that ending was the last straw with ALLEGIANT, then please take a moment and check out this alternate ending I have on my blog, as it has definitely made me feel so much better and more complete to walk away from this series with brighter thoughts!
rows.title: Who wants an ALTERNATE ENDING???? I DO, I DO!

rows.text:
I'm guessing Chichton would be pretty pissed that this was released with his name on it!

Warning: All kinds of details/spoilers if you're worried about that sort of thing because it's necessary to show the ridiculousness of this book so read either this review or the actual book at your peril!

Michael Crichton made a great career of producing incredible ideas with often mediocre characterization but although his characters were little more than stereotypes, the stories always had a logic to them. They made sense. This book does not make sense much of the time but I'm giving Crichton the benefit of the doubt and attributing this to Richard Preston. I have not read Preston's previous works of fiction and this book is not encouraging me to do so.
It starts with earnest grad student Peter being informed of the suspicious death of his brother Eric just prior to Peter and his fellow grad student scientists heading to Hawaii for job interviews with the company Eric worked for. He goes to Hawaii ahead of his classmates to investigate his brother's death(you know, as you do as a grad student) despite getting a text from his brother literally saying "Don't come!" While at the police station he is presented with video evidence that strongly implicates Alyson, vice president of his brother's employer Nanigen, so he naturally he decides to keep that and the preceding text to himself. To show that the author at least knows this doesn't make sense, he has the character repeatedly ask himself "Why did I do that?". His answer to himself?...."because we always looked out for each other as brothers." Wait...what? Not telling the police that you can identify someone who may have had something to do with your own brother's death is looking out for the dead sibling? I can only hope at this point to never become someone that Richard Preston wants to look out for!

But now we have to go with the premise that the genius scientist-grad student is also an idiot. He is then conveniently and magically(!) given recordings from the Alyson's cell phone and that confirm his worst fears again; she and her boss were involved in his brother's death! So now, armed with two forms of overwhelming recorded evidence and a text, he is more determined than ever to NOT involve law enforcement! Instead he devises a plan where he will trick the baddies into confessing during a guided tour so all the other visiting grad students will hear it and I guess, not having a brother to protect, go to the police in his stead.

The students embark on the guided tour designed to attract them to work for Nanigen during which Vin Drake, head Poo bah, explains that the Tensor Generator can shrink stuff. How cool! Still. not particularly wowed by this since they killed his brother and all, Peter subtly makes it known to the ne'er do wells that he is aware of the truth without actually saying anything specific in front of the tour group. You know, gotta protect his bro! Not pleased with this, Drake stops the tour and with everyone looking on, invites Peter to join him in another room where he proceeds to beat the crap out him. Not sure how Drake planned on explaining the wounds or the absence of the potentially never to be seen again Peter but it turns out since the author had other plans, the logic of that was never necessary to address. Problem solved!

Anyway, Peter succeeds beyond anyone's wildest dreams and the bad guy confesses over the loud speakers for the students and probably all kinds of employees hear as well! Woo Hoo! Book over in just a few pages! Oh, wait....there's more. Drake fakes an emergency and then runs into the room full of people that just heard him confess to killing his partner, Eric (and he's aware that they heard him!) in cold blood and says something along the lines of, "Come with me, I'll save you from this emergency involving dangerous robots!" So this room full of scientific geniuses unanimously decide to pick up their suddenly massively beaten colleague and follow the confessed killer and probable beater wherever he wants to take them without a word...instead of just going out the way they came. In fact other employees are running past them going the opposite direction literally yelling " Get outside!" but they follow Drake further into the building anyway. Free thinkers!

Drake leads them into a room clearly marked Tensor Generator(where have we heard that before?) and tricks them into standing on wacky hexagons on the floor by saying "Stand there, you'll be safe!" No one notices that the now exposed killer isn't trying to save himself by standing on a hex. He tells them those hexes are robot free zones or something and that is simply enough. He then leaves for the control room and locks them in. Then he opens the door again briefly and throws a Nanigen employee from the control room in with the students simply barking "Help them!" and then locks the door once more. He next appears in the window of the control booth obviously at the controls.

Now why this employee has no idea what to do during an emergency or what the alarms mean is curious to say the least. Why didn't he run screaming form the building like all those employees the group passed on the way to the Tensor Generator room? He seems to know that Drake means to shrink them all but doesn't seem to mind. That he would have no issue with being shrunken with the students during an emergency is weird since later it is revealed that as operator of that machine, he knew that prolonged shrunkeness would kill you and he had no way of knowing when or if the emergency would be resolved in a timely manner so this is also curious....or stupid. This book is populated by dim bulbs. Or as the incredibly hip Mr Drake said at one point, "Numskulls". Really? We're still saying that are we?

But the technician/corporate stooge/idiot rushes to a hex leading everyone to think that's where they need to be so they all position themselves. Then, in an unknown-emergency-inspired fit of kleptomania, grad student Karen King runs to retrieve a backpack from a wall full of them that she happens to notice hanging from a hook and then rushes back to her hex. No one has been told what is in the backpack or why it is there so it must have had a nice Hello Kitty design on it or something and she likey! And you can't say "Hey, she got the bag because she assumed it had post shrink tools or something!" because she didn't know she was about to be reduced to an insect. She thought she was standing there to be safe from robots. So why would she steal a backpack....in front of everybody?

Wammo! Tiny people ensue! Drake returns and decides to go all sadistic and kill them by feeding them to a snake. The idea being that there would be no evidence if they are consumed. I've never had a snake but it seems odd that a 4-5 foot snake that is used to eating rats would be interested in basically a half inch insect but the book says it is so who am I ask contradict it? 'Luckily', one of the grad students brought a compound to show off with in hopes of getting hired and slips it to Peter as he is thrown in with the reptile 'hoping' it will help fend off the snake. Now I could go all nit-picky and ask whether since they had been reduced to, I dunno, 1/1000000th of their original size to the point that they couldn't be heard and had nearly no mass, that the 'Hexanol' used to save Peter from the snake would probably have no effect since it was likewise reduced but I don't really know the science. It just seems logical. Still, I'm willing to concede the science fiction part of these types of stories as long as they don't break the rules they set for themselves in the context of the tale. So I plug onward!

Aaaaanyway, Unfortunately Drake is called away before he can actually see Peter get eaten by the reptile in a convoluted scene where an Ensign from the Coast Guard shows up to inquire about moving the boat Eric was seen jumping to his death from to a different boat yard for some reason. You see, they need the dead owner's approval and for that they go to his old job....like you do, Instead of the police or family. Maybe the Ensign is also Eric's brother and wants to protect him. I don't know if that is how it's done but it seems like a pretty lousy way to secure evidence to me. Anyway, while there as a device to get Drake out of the room, he lets loose with a torrent of information about what the cops are doing and where they are in their investigation. He even is able to say that the police weren't looking for a specific items on the boat! Apparently the HPD not only immediately notify the Coast Guard of all their progress during an investigation, they even notify all of the lowly Ensigns(No disrespect. Thank you for your service!)! Heck, he even knows that the cops will be at that same facility ANY SECOND to search his office! Who needs a scanner when you have the Coast Guard? The Ensign is invited by Drake to stay until the police arrive and even come along when they search Eric's office. Not sure if the cops want an extra CG member in the room when they are investigating but the Ensign thinks it's a good idea so he stays.

Here's how the conversation morphs from the Ensign rightfully not knowing anything to having every detail:

Drake-"I thought the police were still inspecting the boat"
Ensign - "I wouldn't know about that" The police hadn't come to see him, it was the boat yard owner. - doesn't know anything.
Drake - " I heard they were looking for a phone"
Ensign - "Not that I'm aware, I think the police have finished their investigation" - slowly changing from knowing nothing to having inside info.
Ensign - "At least as soon as they complete their inspection of his office" - Now he knows the inner workings of the investigation!
Ensign - " I know they went to (Eric)Jansen's apartment today and they're coming to look at his office here....any minute now." Suddenly he knows everything including time tables for an organization he is not affiliated with to the extent that they pull up seconds later as if his watch had been synchronized to the Honolulu PD directly!!
Another detour into something I can't speak of with confidence but sounds weird is that CFO Alyson is in her office watching the whole thing through the security cameras that are fed to her desk monitor. This seems odd to me but maybe ultra high tech offices now offer security camera access to all of their top employees. I digress.

The conflicted Ms Alyson, who apparently is a proponent of the 'we should all get to murder one person' rule, because she was fine killing Peter's brother HERSELF via remote control now but suddenly has an issue with multiple homicides so she rescues the helpless minis. This will greatly anger Vin the Merciless whom she knows is determined to kill even her if she betrays him but she proceeds to try to save them anyway because as it says in the book "She had to stop him from killing those kids!"...by driving them to the ocean..... to drown them. Wait...what?
Okay to be fair she decides to save them and then while driving her corporate BMW, she decides to kill them again and then when confronted by Drake, she can't do it, so intends to set them free in the nice, safe Hawaiian rain forest full of who knows what kinds of bugs and creatures that eat little things that are half an inch tall.

Meanwhile Drake decides to kill her but then while driving his corporate midnight blue Bentley convertible decides he "couldn't kill her, he couldn't afford to kill her, he needed her on his side" because it'd be too many deaths or disappearances at one time and would provoke a thorough investigation which would likely discover "things that he had done" and so once it's clear that he can't kill her....he kills her.

But before that, while still at the lab, Drake concludes that Alyson must have the shrunken students in her purse so he scrambles to follow her as she leaves the building. He follows her and eventually confronts her in his 'not going to kill her' mode. He deviously manipulates her into letting them go in the woods but they escape by cutting through the bag they are in and fall ....into the woods. Woo! That was close! They were almost left in the woods against their will! Fortunately, they escaped into the woods of their own accord! She panics and despite the fact she was going to leave them there anyway is worried that they will be hurt or even KILLED! Oh, the humanity!

Drake tells her that the only way out now is to make it look like the grads were out drinking and drove off a really high cliff by planting the MANY personal items belonging to the grad students in it that he *surprize* collected at some point while trying to rush out the door and follow her from the corporate building. He had to rush you see, because it was night time and he was going to have to follow her lights so when exactly he had the time to formulate this plan and gather the items is unclear. He is one fast dude! Still not sure exactly why Drake would give a bunch of prospective employees a BENTLEY to drive around in either. I know I've never been given the corporate car while waiting for the results of a job interview but I hear Hawaii is laid back and that's the story he's going with so.......

He then gets all OCD on her about placing the items where each SPECIFIC student would be sitting in the car because he insists they have to 'stage' the crime scene. How he knows which objects belong to which student having only just met them for a few moments before going postal isn't exactly explained. Maybe in the few seconds he had gather all the items he also took time to watch surveillance video so he could jot down what belonged to whom. He is fast, you know. How these items would stay in place as the car plummets of a 600 foot cliff and into the water and how this is supposed to be important to the police when they potentially find the car is also not exactly clear either. Why it wouldn't be weird that the items be there when ALL six bodies are gone seems a little strange and Alyson mentions this but it is a convertible and with the top down anything could happen I guess and whatever, he's an evil guy and they do wacky things, right? RIGHT??!?!? Alyson is not without her wackiness here too. While being scared to death the police will see through this lame ruse, she can't help but be concerned that one of the items belonging to a student used in staging, a belt made from python, is illegal! She must be one heck of a CFO! She is comforted by Drake who assures her it is only illegal in California. Whew! Let's get back to covering up our probable mass murder!

At this point it seems the idea is that with no top to the car, the missing bodies will not be an issue but what do I know? Nothing as it turns out. He has her drive his Bentley convertible to the edge of the cliff and then puts on gloves, I'm assuming because the fact that it's his car and he was just driving it a minute ago, the gloves would eradicate all his fingerprints from every time he's ever driven the car? Or he was afraid of leaving finger prints on Alyson's skull as he kills her, apparently no longer in 'I need her alive and on my side' mode. Because Drake asks her to get back in the car to drive it literally ten feet so they can then push it off the cliff. She says she doesn't want to and would like him to do it but he demurs saying that he would have to move the seat and THEN the police would be suspicious!!!! She accepts this logic easily because everyone knows, once you move a seat, it's set for life!!!! She then gets back in and drives the long ten feet toward the cliff. He then has her PUT UP THE TOP of the convertible, the only thing that would've reasonably explained why none of the bodies were there, to keep the student's items inside! At this point I am rooting for her slow agonizing death myself. Put her out of my misery; she is too stupid to live!

Meanwhile back in the rain forest, the little grad students get the history of the shrinking project from the Nanigen employee and then take stock of what they have to survive. Karen says "I took a backpack before he shrunk us!" raising more questions that no one asks. Again, why did she take the pack if she didn't know she was about to be shrunk? If the Nanigen employee DID know they were going to be shrunk since he directed everyone to get on the hexes, why DIDN'T he take one? Or tell them all to? If Karen DID know she was going to be reduced, why did she return to the hex at all instead of telling everyone else to get off of the ones they were on to save them? Did she really believe Drake was shrinking them to protect them even after so callously admitting to killing Eric and apparently beating Peter to a pulp just prior? Is she stupid or a thief or stupid? Is she related to Alyson? Is the Nanigen employee stupid or just stupid? Tough call!

After this the group embarks on what is basically a paint by numbers sci-fi escapade with crazy monsters and of course the mandatory extremely annoying member of the group that fights with everyone and then turns on them exposing them to danger because he believes that Drake, who has been trying to kill them the entire book, will suddenly want to help him because he's injured. 'I was going to kill you but now that I see you are hurt, let's get you to a doctor!' is what he's presuming. No real need to go into anymore detail than that. It was basically the first third of the book that was so obscenely ridiculous!

Why did I read this thing? I couldn't stop! It was so bad to begin with that I had to see if it ever made sense! Is that so wrong?

But in case you were wondering, it's set up perfectly for a sequel! I can't wait!
rows.title: This book is so bad, I had to finish it!

rows.text:
I learned the hard way from my mistakes that a cheap battery can cost you way more than its worth..Wish I would have never bought this cheap battery even though worked good for 3 flights with Morpiolot battery before it crashed my drone.. This Battery Cost me wrecking my drone losing all power and dropping out of air! Everyone needs to be very aware of what you risk buying this battery and I'm not saying it isn't a good battery or deal, just in my case is a very very poor investment. I want to inform everyone to watch out for certain issues if you buy this battery so you don't have the same problems I did. Because normally the drone will return to home or Auto land but if you get a defect like I got in battery none of this works and the drone just falls out of air and Morpilot don't care and address any of it so far in my emails as of 8-15-2016
This is my story and its long so if you don't have time, dont' read it.
Extremely upset about the quality control on the battery I received. I noticed one of the gold contacts was very slightly crimped when received the battery. This was where I should of immediately sent it back for a full refund! So anyone else out there, dont' do this and use battery if arrives or is hard to put on. I don't want it to cost you your drone like it did me. My mistake. I thought that is wasn't super bad crimp or bend so though would just see if went on or in drone compartment and worked ok, It really was slight so would probably work. so tried to put it in my drone. It wouldn't go in the compartment and connect to where I could close the back battery latch on the Yuneec drone. I noticed other reviews said it was snug so I pushed and wiggled trying to get it to go over gold contact points in drone heard a pop like noise at same time it finally went on good. I thought well it was snug but at least it went on. I took the battery back out because was a worried plugs on Yunnec drone contact points might not be able to take that but they looked good from outside. After taking it off and trying it 2nd time it seemed to go on much easier and felt satisfied with the results. It must of bent it straight so would work great and I was good to go now! I thought, (boy was I wrong!) I must of bent that slight crimp straight so it would go on good and seemed to work very good now. I looked in compartment and the contacts where still showing so thought must be ok and it goes on easy now. I went out and tested it flying and I would say it does indeed seem to last longer than the stock battery or just as good, so was impressed by this. I flew it twice and every time was greatly impressed, also used the stock yuneec battery about 3 times during this period and I didn't time it but would say it seemed slightly less, so there battery really can be good. On the 3rd flight my drone was up about 5 minutes from a fully charged battery when the voltage dropped sharply after doing many maneuvers then the low battery warning hit screen at same time drone completely quit flying and plunged to the ground!!! I was super super upset! I paid $1350 for this drone when they where very expensive and didn't need a cheap battery! I thought the battery was bad for sure!!!! This however was not true completely. I 1st had to find the drone that went down over a heavily wooded area. I thought I lost drone after about 3.5 hrs of searching. I went back over areas previously had looked but finally found it. It had crashed through the trees broke all the props and completely ruined my camera I had just previously sent in to Yuneec for repairs and had only got a few flight on it with new repaired camera to find it totally destroyed from the crash again!!!.Yuneec is a great company and repaired the camera for free the 1st time and I think there a great company. It also damaged the landing gear in crashing through trees. I have no idea what internal damage it did to drone. I pulled out the battery to discover that one of the gold leads had been completely disconnected during flight in the Yuneec battery compartment and had dislodged from its mount and was sitting in back of drone!!. I knew for sure because of very poor quality control On MorPilots end that I had inadvertently pushed the plug apparently lose inside Yuneec slightly lose even though battery was still going over it much easier after 1st try. I flew it several times like that with no problem and used Yuneec batteries on several flights with it like this with no problem. I am convinced that the MorPilot battery not fitting on there good caused it to dislodge one of the plugs inside the Yuneec battery hatch for sure because no Yuneec Battery ever was so hard to push in and mount on Yuneed Drone as the Morpilot battery. Also it verified it was indeed the battery that caused it because the same exact contact on Yuneec drone is what was loose and not in battery on the same place the Morpilot battery connection was crimped on. If I had it to do over again there is no way I would have tried to use this battery in the Yuneec drone!!. I will admit I thought the battery was bad and not holding its charge but this is not true. The real problem is that the design flaw caused the Yuneec drone plug side (because was hard to push on) it came lose very slightly and must of wiggled loose over time. I was very upset at the time but trying to be more neutral in observing what happened now. I think Yuneec should make there contact points more secure but at same time I know for sure the Morpilot battery being slightly crimped on one lead caused me to crash the drone and totally destroy camera and rip it off the camera completely and destroy the blades from falling through trees and scraping up body of drone, even though the drone body seems to be intact form such a fall as far as main body and have no idea why it only slightly cracked the landing gear. This bird is a tough bird and trees help soften the crash. I have no idea why the body survived falling approximately 150 feet through trees. I know believe the trees saved it from more damage. I will say that maybe I got a bad batch with poor quality control and most are good batteries, and I will admit it worked well when it did work. And because I see allot of good reviews I do believe it works well allot of times, when it works it probably does work better than stock. But there is no way its worth the price of losing the whole drone because they do not make sure the contacts are not perfectly round and this one had a very small crimp on one of the contacts that I am convinced caused the loss of my drone and looking for it for 3.5 hrs and I am very upset my camera is completely destroyed and I have no idea what kind of internal damage was done to the drone yet so I can't comment on that yet. I hope its ok but do not know at this time. The body looks ok and looks way better than I thought it would from crash despite ruining my camera. However upon removal of the Morpilot battery and seeing 1 lead had somehow wiggled lose in flight and somehow from the crash that plug was completely dislodged now and had ended up further back in drone completely out of its holding bracket I'm convinced if I had never bought this cheap battery my drone would still be flying great in the air today!!.
