HERE IS THE BIGGEST WARNING OF WHY I AM VERY UPSET THOUGH. My Yuneec IS NOT COVERED UNDER WARANTY IF CRASH IS CAUSED BY A BATTERY OTHER THAN A YUNEEC BATTERY!!!! YOU HAVE TO ALL KNOW THIS!!! and they informed me if I was using a battery other than there battery NOTHING WOULD BE COVERED UNDER WARRANTY FROM USING ANOTHER MANUFACTURES BATTERY CAUSING DAMAGE TO DRONE!!!!. if you want to risk totally losing your drone and voiding your warranty!!!! and just hope you get a good battery from Morpilot good luck. I'm not trying to rip this company up or say they make bad products or don't have good batteries, but the fact Yuneec WILL COVER NOTHING NOW UNDER WARRENTY DO TO FACT I WAS USING ANOTHER MANUFACTURES BATTERY LEAVES ME WITH FULL BILL OF CRASH AND FIXING IT!!!! The only way I know to really check if connections are safe after hard to install is take drone apart. And that voids your warranty and if you send it in to see if it is ok into Yuneec, well Yuneec can't be responsible for damage from something that is not there product. Yuneec has to charge you for all repairs and checks because was not caused by there battery. That is only fair. That is only fair on there part I'm sure. I feel like your playing the lottery and your whole drone is at stake and you could lose it all. If Morpilot had better quality control and you didn't have to worry about this kind of issue it might be worth it. I don't like to live and learn from such expensive mistakes. If I had it to do over again I would send it back when saw the small crimp on one of connection points. It might be bad luck that I had such a horrible experience with there product that might otherwise be ok. BUT ITS NOT WORTH VOIDING MY WARANTY! AND ME GETTING STUCK WITH FULL BILL WHEN MY DRONE GOES DOWN DO TO A very small design flaw on one of there batteries. Its just bad luck it did not completely break lose so it seemed ok and connected and flew find until wiggled out. If it had not had some kind of connection good enough to fly and just not started up there is no way I could have taken off and had this horrible catastrophe happen to me. I was short on money thinking there cheaper and last longer than Yuneec batteries and I'm not rich so worth going with Morpilot but it cost me having my warranty completely voided and me having to figure out when I can get money to get a new camera (AGAIN!) and what I should do to get my lead fixed and not knowing if any other damage was done inside I can't tell for sure. I can't afford all this kind of hassle just to think I'm getting a cheap good deal on a battery. Very upset!!. Already lost lots of great flying time from just getting camera fixed for free from Yuneec 1st time and they did it for free 1st time! but now its crashed and camera is not under warranty with Morpilot battery or the damaged it caused to my drone. Very Very Very upsetting. Drones are very expensive and this kind of incident makes you wonder if a few more minutes of flying time is worth voiding your whole Warranty and having to fix everything and dig up cash to fix this big mess!! I have no idea!!! Very frustrated about the whole Morepilot experience even though to be completely honest I think if get a good battery and are lucky it will probably be happy with your purchase. I don't want to run the company completely into the ground because of my own experience but you have to be honest and let people know the full ramifications of possible problems of using batteries that Yuneec will not warranty, it don't just effect the warranty of the battery it effects the whole drone when it crashes!!! If you do decide to go with this battery, make very sure it goes on easy. I can't say strongly enough if its hard to get the Morpilot battery on or to get on don't trust it will be ok even if it seems ok. After I got it on it must of bent the crimp enough to go on easy because it went on easier after 1st problem but you really needed to take the whole drone apart and make sure the Yuneec connection points have not become dislodged. And this is a very time consuming hard process and will void your warranty also. So then I would suggest you send drone to Yuneec to inspect but when you tell them I'm not sure the leads are in place on Yuneec anymore and I was using a Morpilot battery and they will probably make you pay for shipping both ways and full cost of repairs, so even if you don't crash the drone if it is hard to get the morpilot battery on and your trying to be safe it will still cost you way more than any money you save!!. I guess somehow it got loose on one lead on my drone even though visually it looked ok I'm very confident I jammed it lose somehow even though flew ok many times and it eventually worked lose out of battery causing entire drone to all out of the air.. Very very upsetting!!. I don't know how you will tell if it knocked a battery connector lose or not because I checked and until the drone crashed, had no idea there was a problem and was impressed with what the battery was doing for my flights. From the outside the lead seemed ok but after crash it was apparent it had been jammed lose from pushing too hard to get the Morpilot battery to fit my battery compartment. I can't blame Morpilot completely, but do mostly because it would have never happened if hadn't got this battery. I'm not sure how I would have figured that out? so hard to blame myself either. . This is my honest opinion of Morpilot Batteries. You might like them and if your very unlucky it will cost you way more than its worth to try and save a little money on these batteries. Or is might just be very bad luck on my part, I have no idea if Yuneec might have a few issues with some of there batteries doing the same thing. But at least!!! my WARANTY IS NOT VOIDED!!!! BE AWARE!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Know the full ramifications of what is going on and the reason I'm posting this to Amazon is if it saves one drone from crashing its worth it to me to write this!!.I want others to learn from my mistakes so they don't learn the hard way like I did. ANYONE OUT THERE IF YOUR MORPILOT BATTERY DOES NOT SLIP ON EASY, DO NOT USE IT! AND DO NOT TRUST DRONE IS SAFE TO USE WITH ANY BATTERY NOW UNTIL IT IS FULLY INSPECTED!!! AND BE PREPARED TO SPEND ALLOT OF MONEY AND TIME SHIPPING IT BACK AND FORTH FROM YUNEEC OR FIX IT YOURSELF AND VOID WARANTY!! AND HOPE YOU KNOW WHAT YOUR DOING!!. I AM CONTEMPLATING FIXING IT MYSELF as far as trying to figure out how to take body apart and put lead back in place and see if drone still works, there is no way I can fix the camera for sure, have to buy a whole new unit now and new blades, I CAN'T AFFORD THIS KIND OF CRAP HAPPEING AND NEITHER CAN YOU!! BUYER BE WARE. MAY OR MAY NOT WORK FOR YOU!!! PLEASE PLEASE! MAKE SURE BATTERY GOES ON EASY OR DON'T FORCE IT!! OR DON'T TRY TOO HARD TO MAKE IT WORK. JUST SEND IT BACK!!! If it goes on easy you hopefully are fine. if its kind of hard to put on 1st time and then easy after that. DON'T TRUST YOUR OK!! YOUR NOT!! MINE DROPPED OUT OF AIR AND WORKED ITS WAY LOSE IN AIR SOMEHOW!!!, my opinion is, with only 3 fights to say, it does indeed seem to last longer than the Yuneec batteries or just as long but wasn't worth it at all in my case. This is my very honest opinion. please make informed decisions with honest peoples experiences and don't trust that because reviews show a bunch of good experiences that you cannot receive a faulty product. Be fully informed and you will have a better chance of having a good experience. Based on my honest true short lived experience, all I can say is the battery appears to be good and do what company says if it slips on easy over your connection points in Yuneec drone, but if it is hard to put on your drone, Please send it back! it is NOT worth it!!! And keep in mind, IS THIS BATTERY WORTH LOSING MY DRONE AND VOIDING MY WARANTY? make sure you get a good one or DON'T BUY OR RISK IT AT ALL!!! I've been working with Morpilot customer service and they get mad because I can't understand them and they will not address my issues. They refuse to address the fact I lost my drone and will never reply to pages of emails I sent on this asking them if they have anything to say about it. This is and exact copies of what they say back. to give you an idea how they work on some of there replies.

Morpilot customer service reply

This photo have showed I HAD SENT THE REPLACEMRNT BEFORE YOU REPLIED TO ME.I do not need to lie.If you think I am a Chinese trying to talk in English,and you want to solve the problem,why do not you speak in chinese?If you do want slove it ,just be polit and patient .As the regulation of Amazon ,we are responsible for the cost of battery.Again,This kind of battery is Li-PO battery which have been attested by CE and UL.
Thank you again.

Wow!!! they claim they have sent me a new battery! and will not address tons of questions on how they feel about the fact there battery crashed my drone. I dont' know where this person is from but his image had Chinese writing on it and I couldn't open it and I had hard time following what they say? Is it just me or do you understand this guy? They refuse to address anything they don't want to deal with. I don't like there customer service but in all fairness you might get a good battery and be completely satisfied. But I really really don't like my experience with them. He still hasn't sent me tracking number or answered any of my other questions and I am very unhappy about my experience but in all fairness you might have a great experience. Wow I'm getting possibly a new battery to replace my faulty battery. Wow. even though my drone that cost me 1350.00 dollars crashed and no matter how many time I report this to them they will not address that part.. Buyer be WARE!!!!!! .. may or may not work for you..

I will update this review when I get drone fixed and see what new battery does, it might work great. I hope it is one of the better batteries and I just had bad luck. Oh well, live and learn. I just wish it hadn't cost me such to figure all this out and crash drone. Please Know this potential pitfall when getting any battery for your drone and make sure it slides on easy and is not defective.

That is the lesson I learned the hard way.

here is another response from them exact copy

Morpilot response

Dear friend ,
Sorry to trouble you again.We are also sorry for the worries we brought to you.We have send the reolacement to you.Our product have been tested . It is probably that the battery have a strong impact in transit, causing internal damage. Our goal is to offer high-quality products and good service,which might take a long time to improve.As there are some insignificance,we try our best to rectify it.Just hope you customer would be satisfied.What’s more, as a customer service,we are committed to solve customers’ problems,and your satisfaction is the encouragement to us .We are wondering if you have other question needed to be solved?
Thank you for your understanding!
Here is the Specification:

• Battery Type: Lithium Polymer (LiPO Battery)
• C Rate: 10C
• Volts: 11.1V
• Capacity: 6300mAh
• Cell Count: 3S
• Cell Configuration: 3S1P
• Continuous Discharge: 10C (64A)
• Max Burst Rate: 20C (128A)
• Max Volts per Cell: 4.2V
• Max Volts per Pack: 12.6V
• Min Volts per Pack: 9V
• Charge Rate: 1C (6.4A)
• Dimensions: 179.5 x 50.5 x 33.5 mm / 7.1 x 1.9 x 1.3 in
• Watt Hours: 66.6
• Weight:14.8 oz (420 g)
• Energy Density: 158.6 WH/KG

Hope it makes sense
END OF Morpilot response.

I guess there trying. but is sure is hard for me to understand them completely. Oh well, good luck to all of you out there, hope you have a great experience with there batteries, I did up to a point and hope and will make sure there new replacement battery has good perfectly round connection points after I fix my drone I guess.

I will try to be fair with the actual new replacement battery if it works good after fix drone. To be honest I think there batteries if not damaged should be as good or better than yuneec. but keep in mind. voids your warranty! so be ware! Trying to be fair after all this stress of crashing drone. I will try to be more fair and just judge the new battery and do a different review later maybe. Still upset about what happened at moment. Its hard not to be upset about drone crashing, just need to let you all try to look out for this potential problem so don't happen to you I guess.

update:8-16-2016
Morpilot battery compared to Yunnec battery does not slide over terminals easy, can see now that drone is apart. I believe it is slightly larger than yuneec battery because Yuneec battery always goes on much smoother. Morpilot harder to fit in slot like others have said. Can see with my terminal back in place the Morpilot battery is a tighter fit and this can cause it to force terminal loose potentially if it is hard to plug into the yuneec battery compartment. Much easier to see how this works with drone apart. Everyone check this to make sure its not an issue Please!!! If the Morpilot battery is hard to slide over your terminals it is potentially unsafe in my opinion. So check it to fly safely. Yuneec battery has more wiggle room to fit over the battery terminals inside the drone compartment on my drone for sure and is a much better fit.
rows.title: Wish I would have never bought this cheap battery even though worked good for 3 flights with Morpiolot battery before it crashed

rows.text:
John Romita Sr. takes over art duties, and the series continues to modernize. This is the era I love most. Romita’s best art is in the first half of this omnibus, but it is great all the way though. The series blends fun and hardships far better in this era. This series features the debuts of the Rhino, the Shocker, and the Kingpin! It also has some amazing returns from Doctor Octopus, the Vulture, and Mysterio!

Amazing Spider-Man #39 ⧫ 4 Stars “How Green Was My Goblin!”
I forgot how quickly Harry and Gwen relax once Romita shows up. The college issues are really more defined from here than earlier. Also, the Green Goblin returns, and he is shockingly revealed! At least, he was. It’s still a little silly, but this is a classic.

Amazing Spider-Man #40 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “Spidey Saves the Day!”
This is a climactic fight that has its own level of silliness, though what really bogs it down is there is way too much exposition and dialogue. Stan needed to calm down. Also, this begins the Goblin’s amnesia that will influence his status quo for this era. Also, it’s not the last amnesia…

Amazing Spider-Man #41 ⧫ 4 Stars “The Horns of the Rhino!”
The first original villain of this era is the Rhino! He’s pretty fun, and I think his build up and continuing presence over the next few issues are what distinguish him as a threat and make him memorable. Pete and Betty meet again and officially end their relationship on a more positive note. Also, John Jameson returns to become more of a recurring supporting character. This continues the more lax social bleedover that makes this era of Spider-Man feel much more modern.

Amazing Spider-Man #42 ⧫ 4 Stars “The Birth of a Super-Hero!”
So, this issue’s villain is the worst part of it. Everything else surrounding that is far more significant, including the college crew continuing to form. Oh, and possibly the most iconic Spider-Man panel ever: “Face it, tiger… You just hit the jackpot!” Yep, the actual intro of the amazing Mary Jane!

Amazing Spider-Man #43 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “Rhino on the Rampage!”
Mary Jane immediately sets herself apart, and this issue even works with a retcon that comes later. This wraps up the Rhino and preps the return of the Lizard. Why so highly rated? I think Romita’s style is really beginning to click, though his paneling will get better. It’s just a good handoff that feels like what this era is remembered for.

Amazing Spider-Man Annual #3 ⧫ 2 Stars “...To Become An Avenger!”
Ugh, the Avengers are jerks in this, and it’s just not a very fun issue to read with so much procedure and legalism. Some have pointed out that the moment of understanding Spider-Man has with the Hulk is great, but that’s one page. I wouldn’t join the Avengers either.

Amazing Spider-Man #44 ⧫ 4 Stars “Where Crawls the Lizard!”
The Lizard returns as Mary Jane joins the college crowd! It’s a little more like the Ditko era in the ironic downer ending of this and the next Lizard story. The continued formation of the supporting cast, both the college crowd and Conners’ family, continue to fuel my enjoyment of this era.

Amazing Spider-Man #45 ⧫ 4 Stars “Spidey Smashes Out!”
Spider-Man defeats the Lizard in an original way and just more good fun from the supporting cast! From here on, the action panels are large and intricate. I love the way Romita distinguishes distinct action from the close up dialogue scenes. It’s something that he continues for the rest of the run.

Amazing Spider-Man #46 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “The Sinister Shocker!”
The second new villain of the era, the Shocker has become something of a joke in recent years, but he’s rather cool here. I like how well thought out the suit is and the fact that the combat is very well thought out. Also, there’s been a Bugle reported named Foswell who’s become suspicious of Peter who is also dealt with cleverly in this issue.

Amazing Spider-Man #47 ⧫ 4 Stars “In the Hands of the Hunter!”
This is an iconic issue because of the hangout scene, a defining feature of the era, and Kraven’s attack on the hangout. Also, Kraven has laser nipples… Just touching on the important stuff.

Amazing Spider-Man #48 ⧫ 4 Stars “The Wings of the Vulture!”
I might just have to give it up and say the Vulture is one of my favorite villains. even here with the silly new vulture, I rather like him. The snow really helps distinguish this issue, though it begins the disturbing trend of villains not checking if Spider-Man is dead and just assuming it. It’s annoying, but I forgive it because I love everything else.

Amazing Spider-Man #49 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “From the Depths of Defeat!”
Believe it or not, this is the first time Spidey’s faced two major supervillains at the same time, unless you count the Goblin and Crime-Master back in issues 26-27. Thus, it’s more fighting, which aren’t my favorite issues, but this one is unique enough.

Amazing Spider-Man #50 ⧫ 5 Stars “Spider-Man No More!”
This issue is iconic, and I can’t really disagree. Both the cover and the page #8 splash have been reproduced countless times. It does feel a little out of nowhere because it’s touching on the endings from issues #43-45 and #47, not the last few issues since Spider-Man has been too hurt/injured. So, it just feels a little weird. Otherwise, it’s awesome, and it ends on the introduction of the Kingpin! In other words, it holds up as the best issue since #33.

Amazing Spider-Man #51 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “In the Clutches of the Kingpin!”
This issue establishes Kingpin as a real threat. It’s back to the blend of personal and superheroes, and I think this continues in the fine tradition of early Spidey three-parters.

Amazing Spider-Man #52 ⧫ 4 Stars “To Die a Hero!”
So, the biggest issue here is Kingpin not removing Spider-Man’s mask and using a death trap. It’s some sillier 60s stuff that is a little out of place. I suppose one could say the Kingpin is arrogant and wants their drowning deaths to look like an accident, but it is the weakest part of the trilogy. It does have some good J.J.J. and Foswell stuff.

Amazing Spider-Man Annual #4 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “The Web and the Flame!”
Okay, this is another use of the movie making trap trope, but it also displays what I think is the first use of Spider-Man villains having backup plans. Also, I love the Human Torch.

Amazing Spider-Man #53 ⧫ 4 Stars “Enter: Dr. Octopus”
Some good, classic Spider-Man here with Spidey’s personal life leading into his superhero life. Also, there’s some Professor Warren and Gwen interaction that read very differently based on some later events.

Amazing Spider-Man #54 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “The Tentacles and the Trap!”
The series continues and gets way more fun. Some people like to make fun of this issue, but I actually love how Doc Ock showing up at Peter’s home goes. It feels like a more committed version of the Goblin’s visit in #39.

Amazing Spider-Man #55 ⧫ 4 Stars “Dock Ock Wins!”
I feel like I say a lot of similar things for this era: great art, good blend of plots, and cool villains. This one has all that with a little complication at the end that I’ll talk about next time.

Amazing Spider-Man #56 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “Disaster!”
I gave some crap for the Goblin amnesia, but I like the Spider-Man loss of identity two-parter. It allows a look at what a lonely existence Spider-Man would have without the others as well as provides some drama.

Amazing Spider-Man #57 ⧫ 4 Stars “The Coming of Ka-zar!”
The J. Jonah stuff is a little repetitive, but otherwise, I like the conclusion to the amnesia arc. Ka-Zar is a logical guest start and doesn’t feel too intrusive.

Amazing Spider-Man #58 ⧫ 2.5 Stars “To Kill A Spider-Man!”
Ugh, I hate Spider-Slayers, and this is no different. The one thing that’s changed is that Smythe is crazy now. The continuing supporting cast stuff is the main reason to read this.

Amazing Spider-Man #59 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “The Brand of the Brainwasher!”
This story gets some crap because it is a little silly, but I rather like it. The epilogue to the amnesia storyline starts the issue, and I appreciate the character development for Peter, Captain Stacy, and Gwen. For a silly story, it has a killer cliffhanger.

Amazing Spider-Man #60 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “O, Bitter Victory!”
This is also the closest that the series has gotten to refuting the Mary Jane retcon, and Mary Jane’s movements in this storyline are actually rather confusing. She just disappears after talking to Captain Stacy and shows up next issue like nothing happened. Peter and Gwen fall out here for relatively good reasons that turn a little sour next issue.

Amazing Spider-Man #61 ⧫ 3 Stars “What a Tangled Web We Weave...!”
The series ends weaker than I’d like with more deathtraps from Kingpin and Peter being an idiot again. Just explain to Gwen!

Amazing Spider-Man #62 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “Make Way for... Medusa!”
Romita wanted to draw Medusa, so he concocted a fun little plot to do it. It’s fun

Spectacular Spider-Man Magazine #1
“Lo, This Monster” ⧫ 2.5 Stars
This story is very out of place in the Omnibus as it completely ignores the state of Gwen and Peter’s relationship. However, I can’t seem to find anywhere that it could really go… Honestly, this pseudo-Frankenstein political drama is only worth reading for the art.

Amazing Spider-Man #63 ⧫ 4 Stars “Wings in the Night!”
Vulture vs. Vulture with Spidey in the middle. I’m actually really glad the original Vulture returned. Though his escape is… shaky. It creates a cool story that escapes the diminish returns that villains can have.

Amazing Spider-Man #64 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “The Vulture's Prey”
Ugh, I’m tempted to rate this down for MJ’s terrible haircut. The rest of this is great, though. The Bugle setting and the cliffhanger ending are classic.

Amazing Spider-Man #65 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “The Impossible Escape!”
Spider-Man and Captain Stacy escape prison. It’s another creative setup with fantastic use of setting and tone.

Amazing Spider-Man #66 ⧫ 4 Stars “The Madness Of Mysterio!”
I’ve given some crap to Mysterio’s previous plans, but I like this one. Also, another great cliffhanger.

Amazing Spider-Man #67 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “To Squash A Spider!”
Cool setup, cool conclusion. True, Mysterio’s skill is still questionable, but it looks great!

Spectacular Spider-Man Magazine #2 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “The Goblin lives!”
Infinitely better than the first and a somewhat unique Goblin story, though it doesn’t stand out as much as many others. This is the last of the magazine format, and I think it’s for the best as it seems to mostly need to have disposable stories.

Amazing Spider-Man Annual #5 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “The Parents of Peter Parker!”
Peter Parker’s parents become kind of a mess, but this is a neat little story. I am suspicious about how much Peter’s solution will work, but I guess I’m not a spy.
rows.title: Spider-Man at his Most Fun!

rows.text:
Well firstly, I will NOT attempt the usual plot description with THIS film, as THAT would be no easy undertaking, to say the least! Now it isn't that this movie just doesn't have ANY plot. No,quite the contrary in fact,.. . THIS insane &@#%!^& flick actualy seems to have SEVERAL different plots, . .. and a whole bunch of sub-plots inbetween!! Kind of in the same way an insane PERSON might have several diferent and distinct personalities, I supose! And while these manifold scenarios do kind of overlap ( barely anyway ) and feature the same characters, they actualy seem more like distinct and unrelated segments that have been compiled into this one movie, and very loosely conected to each other, instead of a single cohesive premis,.. giving the overal film a kinde of anthological quality ( which, it seems, it may as well have been intended as )! Hell, at one point, near the end of the film, it even dispenses with ANY kind of storyline and goes COMPLETELY off the rail, . . becoming totaly insane and anarchic!!
To enhance the confusion, theres also something of a melding of genres! Drama, porn, horror, biblical epic, art film, .. . this motion picture seems to feature elements of ALL of them, .. yet it's NONE of them ( though it's entirely humorous, .. however in a very warped fashion )!! In short, a very, .. . dissordered movie, to say the least!
However, THIS is a John Waters film, .. . an EARLY John Waters film at that! Not, minde you, that John "Hairspray, Cry Baby, Serial Mom, Ceicel B. Demented" Waters whom you see nowadays on TV talk shows, . . no, I mean that infamous deranged maveric visionary with that seedy, yet strangely exotic, troup of genuine social pariah types from WAY beyond the fringe, and yet unspoild by Hollywood and the mainstream media! This is therefore NOT a motion picture that I recomend for ordinary conventional moviegoers ( NO!! For the love of God, and Hollywood, RETREAT!! STAY AWAY!!! Tis FORBIDEN unto YOU!!! ), .. however, I ABSOLUTELY DO recomend this motion picture to fans of EARLY John Waters ( "Come on in ye sickos!! .. Have at it, by all means, and join the fun!!! ) For THAT rare and special breed of moviegoer does NOT come to a Waters movie admiring and demanding cohesive plot structure! No, what THEY value and treasure in a Waters work are outrageous situations and ideas, .. foul, caustic,and hysterical verbal dialogue, ..and outlandish characters like only HE can dream up! And,sufice it to say, THOSE deviates will NOT be dissatisfied here!
Of course, being a vintage work, this film does lack that unique visual style that Waters later more lavishly budgeted films have ( made in black & white, with a $ 100 budget, and before people like Vince Peranio and Van Smith came into the Waters fold to contribute their creative talents ), .. but none the less, it's cripplingly low budget not withstanding, Waters distinctive brand of creativity and humor is definately here on very raw display ( and THATS something money can't buy anyway, right you sickos ? ), and it does feature some of his most twistedly imaginative concepts and funniest screenwriting!

To begin with, theres this certain 'Lady Divines Calvacade, .. . a kind of mobile menagerie of human exhibits. A "freakshow" of sorts ( remember those ? ). However, hold up gramps, cause THIS is the Calvacade of PERVERSION!! And the human anomolies on dissplay HERE are, in the words of the hysterical master of ceramonies, "Asorted f@&s, $!uT$, and d!k$#!!! Real actual FILTH who've been carefuly screened in order to bring YOU the most flagrant violation of natural law known to man!!!", .. and which "comit acts which would make any decent person recoil in disgust!!!" .. . more specificaly, porn models, sado masochists, underwear fetishists, dopers, kissing "qu##r$$", a girl ecstaticaly whiffing and caresing a well-used bicycle seat, and a "puke eater" who spews into a bucket and "laps it back up for you!! He loves it!!", . .. right, instead of the more traditional dwarves, siamese twins, dog-faced boy, human pincushion and so forth, . . you get the picture ? "What you will see in THIS tent will make you literaly SICK!!!" asures said barker. And where paying spectators marvel, recoil, gag, sneer, scowel, retch, and utter remarks such as .."No decent person would ever act like that!!" .."DECENT!!! HA!! SHE doesn't even KNOW the MEANING of the WORD decent !!!"
The tuor concludes with the shaken spectators being nabed, pistol whiped and robed, .."Kindly hand over all wallets, jewelry, fur items, .. . and any narcotics you might be carying !"

Then this Calvacade scenario is seemingly ditched, and hardly even figuers in the remainder of the film ( getting back to that previously mentioned anthology aspect ), .. and is succeded by what I discern to be kind'a the MAIN premis from here onward, .. . a kind of story about infidelity and retribution, .. yet done Waters-style ( meaning twisted and skewerd ), and with Divine as the jilted wife from hell out for revenge, .. homicidal revenge,at that!!

"OOOOOOOOO, . .. That slimy little pig-$^#%@& and cheesy street walker girlfriend of his!! Niether of them deserve an ounce more air running through their putrid little lungs!! I hope they've had their fun, cause it'll be the LAST fun they'll ever have!!!"

This latter scenario leads to a hillariously inimical and caustic living room squable ... .

"I can only take so much from a common lesbian!!"

"Well, my dear, at least I'm not some bleahced blond hu$$@y who goes around $@r#wing unhired giggalos!!"

"Quiet!! Your speaking to someone who is miles above YOUR element!! I mean, where did you meet ?!! Were you her gym instructer or something ?!!"

.. erupting into a frenzied brutal orgy of murder with a butcher-knife ( inspired by the then recently occured Sharon Tate murder and also kind'a prefiguering the future "slasher" movie trend ) complete with some canibalism for good measure, .. . and followed by a rapturous rave about the incomparible joys acrued from same .. .

"Ohhhhh you cant imagine the thrill it gives when the knife first plunges and the person resists a little!!!! Ohhhhhh I LOVED doing it !!!!!"

Theres also this totaly perplexing ( to myself at least ) subplot which comes utterly out of nowhere, but seems to be a kind of parody of surealist cinema with religious overtones involving an Infant of Prague, .. . what apears to be a midget in a potentate costume???? This is the one part of this film which, I must admit, goes totaly right by me ( though it does have an admitedly dreamy quality ). But, then again, I'm personaly unfamiliar with the catholic faith, which may be the reason that I just dont get it. But anyway ... .

Of cours, one MUST have deviant sexuality, lurid and scandalous, in a Waters flick, particularly an early one. Well, that prerequisite is well-fulfilld by such an escapade, of a pronouncedly sacriligeous character, ocuring in a church, and featuring such dialogue as .. .

"As I continued to pray, I'd picked up a strong sexual vibration from that woman behinde me , .. who was robing me of my prized thoughts and drawing me back into my everyday quest for self gratification!!" ... "She then kissed me as though Christ himself had orderd every move of her experienced tongue!!"

.. . and verging on pornographic! Involving sodomy with a crucifix, . . "rosary job" as it's called in the film ( some years before Linda Blair comited that transgresion in The Exorcist it should be noted )!

Then, there is what could be called the showstoper of the film,.. and posily the most outlandish thing that Mister Waters demented minde has ever given birth to! A truely fantastic, and even horrifying ( however absurd )scene in which Divine is attacked and carnaly ravished, on a living room sofa, by a fifteen foot broild lobster ( thats right, . . a lobster )which looks like it'd escaped from some trashy brade B monster flick from the 1950's, .. and not seeming to enjoy enjoy the experience judging from her anguished blood-curdling shreiks, .. . the monsterous entity grasping her breast with it's enormous pincer while mounting and humping her repeatedly!! This incredible outrage being preceded by a lurid macabre monologue ( deliverd amid a surounding litter of murdered victims on the floor ) about delving utterly and irretreivably into ones "own depravity" and attaining a state of total monsterdome "Your a monster now!!! And only a monster can realize the fulfilment I'm capable of !!!" , .. with absurd, yet very imagistic Godzilla references "You can wipe out entire shoping malls with one stomp of your foot!!! And destroy entire cities with one blast of your fiery breath!!!" ( Divine really should've taken an Oscar for this scene. His talent as an actor is clearly evident here!).
For additional sexual, gross-out shock-gag value, theres even a Divine nude scene ( "Get me my loungewear honey!" ) !!

All the stars of the old Waters realm are here. Besides Divine, theres David Lochary as both the hysterical and previously well-quoted Barker during the Calvacade segment and as Divines cheating and anxiety-ridden boyfriend ( something of a duo-role of sorts, .. though I think it's suposed to be the same character ? The name is the same.)... . "Ohhhhh god!! You cant imagine what it's like!! Every moment she's alive she's getting worse and worse!! I'm afraid she'd kill us both on the spot if she caught us together!!"

Mary Vivian Pearce as his mistress. Sort of a Waters-warped version ( or rather per-version ) of the steriotypical ditzy blonde miss homewrecker .. . "Ohh Bonnie, have you ever killd anyone before ?!" .. "Well no I havent. But it would be something new !"

Edith Massey as a busybody bartender who informs Divine of the affair ( thus sealing the couples mortal doom in the proces ) . ..
"I just thought you'd like to know that your old mans over here with another broad!"

Mink Stole as a saphic seductress, with a blasphemy fetish, who exclusively haunts churches .. . "I'm heaty most places. Like they heard about me over at St. Anthony's, so they always send a nun out to spy on me. But, ya know what? .. I got a nun once, . . and let me tell you that it was JUST what she needed !"

Coockie Meuller as Divines teenaged, doper, whore, and practicaly nudist daughter ( "Divine saw to THAT from the day she was born!" ) who's the frequent subject of her squabbles with Lochary .. . "He's been using you to throw up in my face! He sayes I'm f@#%!^ you up!"
. . "Well then I'm glad you did, that fart!! I couldn't be happier!!"

Even the old Eggman, Pual Swift, from Pink Flamingos putes in a role here as one of Coockies tricks who'm she casualy introduces to her mother while in bed naked together .. . "Oh hi mom. I'd like you to meet Steve. Steve, this is mom!" .. "Hi there!"

And, last but by no means least, in it's sole movie role ( to date ), .. the immortal Lobstora as it'self ?!!
rows.title: Rampageing 15 ft. concupiscent crustatian copulates with 300 lb. drag queen on living room sofa!!! .. Film at eleven!!

rows.text:
***Review posted on The Eater of Books! blog***

The Debutante is Mine by Vivienne Lorret
Book One of the Season's Original series
Publisher: Avon Impulse
Publication Date: April 12, 2016
Rating: 4 stars
Source: Copy sent by the publisher

***Warning: this is an adult book, and for the eyes of mature readers***

Summary (from Goodreads):

From the Season Standard: “A true Season’s Original embodies the class, grace, and style of the ton. Such an honor ensures the recipient her pick of eligible suitors…”

Lilah Appleton’s prospects are looking dim. With one last chance to find a titled husband before she’s forced to wed her wretched cousin, she must make this Season count. Plain, forgettable Lilah must become the Season’s Original. Desperate, she seeks help from the devilishly charming, untitled, and thoroughly unsuitable Jack Marlowe. All she must do now is resist the tempting rogue…

Bastard son and self-made man, Jack Marlowe loathes the aristocracy. When he meets Lilah, he expects her to be like all the other greedy husband-hunters. But she’s far more dangerous. Her alluring smiles and sharp tongue intrigue him. Before he knows it, he agrees to help her find a husband, revealing tricks to ensnare any man. The only problem is, his plan works too well—on him.

When Lilah becomes the belle of the ball, Jack realizes he may lose her forever—unless he can take a chance on love and claim his debutante...

What I Liked:

I received a copy of this book via the Avon Addicts program, sponsored by the publisher! In no way did this affect my opinion of the book.

I've not read a book by Lorret! I've seen her historical romance novels around, and some very positive reviews for her books. I knew that eventually, being the HR lover that I am, I would read one of her books. This one was an excellent start! I adored this sweet, enjoyable novel.

At twenty-three, Lilah Appleton is at her third and most likely final Season. At the end of this Season, if she doesn't land a rich, titled husband, she'll be forced to marry her cousin, in order to keep the family estate/lands/money within the family... as decreed by her controlling, verbally abusive father (who is dead before the beginning of this book). When Lilah meets Jack Marlowe, she knows better than to engage with the likes of him. His reputation is quite known - he likes women, and he makes a lot of money. He's the bastard son of an already rich duke, but he wants nothing to do with his father. What better way to learn how to charm noblemen than to enlist the help of a notorious rake who is estranged from the ton?

Ahhhh! Did I mention that this book was cute? It was! Sweet, cute, adorable, with touches of angst and "real" issues that gave the story and characters depth, but weren't overwhelming. I LOVE that this book is over one hundred pages shorter than your average HR novel. If you're looking for a sweet and steamy HR novel that won't take forever to read, here is one!

On the note of this novel being short - the length of the story did not hinder the development of the story or the characters or the conflict! Lorret does a marvelous job of telling the story and keeping the pacing steady and yet engaging. We know that time passes in this book, even if the book does not have scenes for each day in the hero and heroine's life since meeting. Days and weeks pass in this book, so no need to worry about rushed development!

Lilah is such an adorable protagonist! She is so determined to find a titled gentleman this Season - and with good reason. She has her hopes set on Lord Ellery, who lives close to where her family's estate is (Lilah is currently living with an aunt). But sadly, literally everyone (man or woman) seems to forget Lilah exists, or that they were introduced to Lilah in the past, or that Lilah is in the room too. She thinks of herself as plan and unforgettable. But she has a lot of self-esteem and confidence, and she has a fiery tongue when she isn't stuffing herself behind society rules. I adore her!

But Jack - Jack is easily my favorite character of the book. Oh Jack! He's the only son of a very important and rich duke (who's name and title I've forgotten), and he's the duke's bastard son. The duke married and had eight daughters, all much younger than Jack, and none of which can inherit. For the first ten years of his life, Jack had a very difficult life. His father wouldn't marry his mistress (Jack's mother), so the mistress had to raise her son on her own, until Jack was ten, and she sent him to his father, so that the duke would send Jack to school. And off to Eton Jack went. Jack has held bitterness toward his father for years, even though the duke and duchess want nothing more than for Jack to "come home" and accept his place with them, as heir, and son.

I don't blame Jack! His childhood was rough, and when he left for Eton, he felt terrible for leaving his mother (and he was only ten!). Jack is a charming, wickedly charming young man, and Lilah sees him as a warrior. He is a large guy, and he's a self-made man. He's a curiosity in polite society (as a bastard son), and so he avoids society. Until Lilah, who intrigues him. Suddenly, you can find Jack at all the social events! Which makes for HILARIOUS scenes, trust me.

I love how lighthearted this story is, so funny and cute! Lilah and Jack have a hate-to-love relationship (well, at least on Lilah's side). I liked seeing them dance around each other (so to speak)!

I mentioned Jack's issues with his father - honestly, I loved this "angst" in the story. It's really not that angsty, but I loved it. There were two scenes with Jack and his father that BROKE my heart in pieces - poor Jack! Poor duke! I love how Lorret wrote those scenes. I wanted to give everyone a hug!

You see why I loved Jack, right? He's such a charming alpha, but he has daddy issues. I don't know why that package is so appealing to me, but it is!

The romance is sweet! There is chemistry between Jack and Lilah. Unfortunately there is only one sex scene (booo), but given the length of this novel (less than three hundred pages), that is to be expected, I suppose. Still, there were some subtly steamy scenes in this book! Not many though.

Overall, I really liked this novel. I picked it up on a whim and I regret nothing!

What I Did Not Like:

Obviously I'm going to mention the lack of sex scenes! Of course I am. It's weird because I liked how this book was shorter than your average HR novel, but I definitely wanted more sexy times!

A note that is more to the publisher than author - the print of this paperback was SO small. Like, even smaller than the text size of your average HR novel. I have great vision but I was totally squinting!

Would I Recommend It:

I highly recommend this novel, if you're in the mood for HR but don't have the time for one of those four hundred page bad boys! Length of book almost never bothers me; however, I appreciated the fact that this book was short! It was adorable. And the ebook is very cheap!

Rating:

4 stars. My first venture into Lorret books was a success! I definitely want to continue the series. I believe Wolford (who's name is LIAM!) is next, and then Max/Juliet! I am super SUPER excited for Max/Juliet's book. So many secrets between those two. Need!
rows.title: Review for The Debutante is Mine by Vivienne Lorret

rows.text:
Current DLS:
Stolen Melissa's paper/ Nominated for a Golden Orchid with stolen paper/ Has a weird memory about Ali, which she isn't sure about/Melissa finds out and so do her parents

Spencer: She is talking to a therapist about her life and actually enjoys talking to Dr. Evans a lot. When she comes out of the office, though, her mom tells her that one of her papers are up for this great literary award. Spence feels terrible, because it was Melissa's stolen paper that was nominated and it looks like Melissa knows about that, too! Spence is feeling more and more frustrated as people continue to congratulate her on being nominated. She starts to think that the Rosewood Stalker is `A'. Now, Spencer is freaking out, because she thinks Melissa knows! Spence really did the right thing. When Melissa was being a competitive Bitch with a capital B, Spencer just said she was right and totally shocked/confused Melissa. Gosh, what sweet revenge! After the yearbook meeting during lunch, Spencer looked at her phone and the unopened text from `A' was still there. The night that Ali went missing, Spencer went after her, but she cannot really remember what happened after. It was like a piece of that night is missing from her memory. She has just remembered a memory of Ali laughing nastily and turning away. I wonder if its from that night. Spence's stolen paper has made it to the finals in the Golden Orchid. She suspects that Andrew knows about the stolen paper, because he was asking a lot of suspicious questions about the sources that she had used. Andrew is being creepy watching Spencer's every move like that. `A' wants Spencer to do something for her or else `A' will tell everyone about the stolen paper. `A' is trying to tell Spence that the murderer is herself, but I don't think she gets it yet and I don't think that Spence is the murderer. Spencer is getting little snippets of a lost memory and it seems to suggest that Ali and her were arguing about something. Her family is currently doing the interview for the newspaper about her winning the Golden Orchid. Spence is going to be on the front cover of the paper and `A' isn't sure whether she should tell the whole world or not. Spencer really is over her head in all this mess and Melissa seems to know things that she shouldn't. Spence has gone to see Doctor Evans again. The doctor wants to try to hypnotize her to get the suppressed memory out of her. The memory comes out and its about a fight that her and Ali had on the night that Ali went missing, but Spencer cannot remember the last thing that Ali said to her. Spencer caught someone watching her behind a truck tire. This series is really starting to get creepy! Spencer and her parents are out to dinner to celebrate Spencer's accomplishments. Her parents talk to her about the doctor that she has been seeing and asks her if she feels much better after going to her. Their waiter brings up the Rosewood Stalker, which I'm pretty sure is `A', who I am pretty sure is Mona. Spence's mom tells her about the time a man tried to rob her at gun point with her and Melissa right there with her. Her mom told her that that was the one time that Spencer has had amnesia and Spence sitting there still could not remember that day. Then, all of a sudden, Spencer had a fever and her parents had to miss Melissa's national spelling bee contest. To make sure that Spencer would be okay, Melissa missed many things so that her parents could stay close to Spencer. I think that is the stem of the problem Spencer has with Melissa. After all those years, Melissa hasn't gotten over being second place to Spencer, so she made Spencer feel that way. Oh, boy! I'm glad that I don't have a sister! Spencer is afraid of herself, since now she is convinced that that wasn't her last blackout. While tending her mother's garden, Ian Thomas came over to see Melissa, but stops to talk/flirt with Spencer. Oh, Snap! Melissa told Ian about her therapy sessions and that she had heard Spencer screaming. While in the hot tub, her sister climbs in with her and tells her that she's mad at Dr. Evans. Melissa tells her that the doctor told her that she chooses untrustworthy guys. She thinks that she knows who Ian was cheating with, while they were dating in high school. Spencer asks her what she would do to the girl who cheated with Ian and Melissa says that she might have done something already to her. What if it was Ali? What if Melissa killed Ali? Spencer is getting ready for Mona's party. Her parents show her the front page of the newspaper with her picture on it and that the story is up online.

Current DLS:
Father is living with girlfriend/ Mom kicked her out of the house/Mick isn't speaking to her and neither is her mom/ Tug-of-war between Ezra and Sean

Aria: Is still super upset with Spencer over her lying about Toby after all those years of them being friends. Her mother is still not close to her, but at least she is talking to her. Her father is living with his girlfriend. Her thing with Ezra is over especially since she found out that he had a girlfriend. Hanna is still mad at her for going out with Sean, which she is still doing. Aria ran into Ezra while waiting for Sean to come back to his car to give her a ride home. Ezra handed her an essay on The Scarlet Letter talking about if adultery was okay in some circumstances. She took it, but said that it wasn't ever acceptable. Sean was driving her home, when her dad texted her to talk to him at Hollis. She gets this crazy idea in her head as Sean dropped her off at Hollis. She walked right up to Meredith and painted a big, red A on her dress and stormed out.
After, she had a crazy laugh and felt very satisfied. Warns Em in a voice message that she should talk to her before doing anything, because `A' is capable of really anything. Aria sits down to watch the leaked video with her brother, Mike. Aria tells her mother what she did to Meredith, painting a red A on her dress. Her mother is upset that she did that, but Aria just wanted revenge for her mom not to make it worse. Aria ends up telling her mom that she won't be coming home tomorrow and that seems to make her mom happy since she doesn't want to see Aria for awhile after what she just did to Meredith. After talking to Em, Aria goes over to the vending machine where Hanna and Spencer were talking about Emily. Aria suggests that they look for `A', but Spence said she had to leave and she ran out into the parking lot. Mike asked Aria if she was going to live at Sean's for awhile and she said yes in front of Hanna. Hanna tells her that is relationship suicide and walks away from Aria. Mike tells Aria that Mom told him that Aria knew about the affair. Mike is mad at her for not telling him about it. Aria is settling in at Sean's house in a spare bedroom. Sean's mom is making vegetarian foods, since Sean told his mom that Aria doesn't eat meat. Sean's mother sympathizes with her about the death of Ali, which was surprising. A text from `A' tells Aria to tell Sean about what happened with Ezra. She tries to find Sean, but cannot after seeing that they Rosewood Stalker was spotted at the house next to his. She goes back to her room and finds an email from `A' saying that she wasn't the murderer. `A' says that the murderer wanted something of Ali's and that the person is closer than you think. Aria and Sean have decided to bike around West Rosewood. They come across a cemetery and decide to go in and have a look around. Aria used to come here a lot when she was going through her Goth stage. Aria complains about Sean's stepmother's cleanliness, but he doesn't agree with her. He says tours go through the house everyday and that she works hard to keep everything clean and neat. They cross Ali's grave that Aria didn't know about. They stopped and she thought of the things she wanted to say to her, but couldn't say out loud. They go to leave and Sean makes up a couple game for them. Rabbit. KISS! Aria thinks its stupid. She doesn't want to be with Sean forever. Aria went to Ezra to get a extension on her report and he gave it to her. She tells Ezra about her dad's affair and everything that came with it. Ezra tells her a story about his own family and their problems. She asks Ezra about the girlfriend in New York and he tells her that they had broken up that summer. Aria and Ezra share a passionate kiss, before Aria breaks it off remembering that she is with Sean. `A' leaves her a note telling her that she is always watching, but she also left Aria another clue about which friend is keeping the biggest secret of their life. Aria is on the phone with Sean talking about Mona's party, when Ezra calls her also. Ezra asks her to meet him somewhere and Aria tells him that she will be right over. She hangs up on Sean before answering his question if she was going to Mona's. She arrives at Ezra's and they drink Scotch together. They talk about their families. They kiss and decide to give their relationship a second shot. Aria just had a bad nightmare and woke up next to Ezra. Ezra talks about moving some where else and Aria suggested places where they could go to get away from everything. The cops just busted through Ezra's door and are arresting him. Sean was in the hallway and Aria asked him what he was doing there. Sean got a text from `A' with pictures of Aria and Ezra kissing and he called the police on him. `A' has struck again! Aria is at a diner with all her stuff around her. She calls home to see if she can live there, but no one answers the phone. She called her dad next, but she heard Meredith's voice in the background and hung up on him. Aria thinks about the dream and opens up her laptop and looks through the videos she had made when Ali was still alive. Aria figures out that Hanna has or had a bulimia problem from one of the videos that Ali had pointed out.. Another video revealed that Ali also knew about Em being gay. Another revealed Ali taunting Aria about her secret about her dad. Aria was in the middle of finding out Spencer's secret, when the waitress talks about a boy. Aria figured out that Ali had been with Ian!!!

Current DLS:
Puking/Father hasn't spoken to her since Foxy/ Mona isn't her friend anymore/ Uninvited to Mona's party/Knows who `A' is

Hanna: Two evil girls at the school has spread rumors about Hanna puking around the school, but she is so over it. Hanna comes home to change clothes and go out to have dinner with Mona, but finds out that she's under investigation. All her old friends are at her house with the police out back. When she walks through the house, her mother tells her that she doesn't have to volunteer at the burn clinic anymore, which leaves Hanna surprised. She is still upset that her father doesn't want to speak/hear/see her anymore. Someone leaked out a video to the press with the girls talking about text-messages. The video was recorded back when Ali went missing. The police think that Alison's killer is still in Rosewood that the person lives in Rosewood and has been since the beginning. Mona calls and is extremely upset to find out that Hanna is with her old friends, but the officer takes her phone before she could really explain to Mona what was going on. The officer brings up Toby's suicide and asks the girls what was Ali's secret. The girls all get a message from `A' saying not to tell anyone about her. While at the mall waiting for Mona to call about her dress fitting, Hanna finds Mona with the two mean girls from school. Mon and Hanna get into a mock-fight. Mona said that the court was going to be bigger, because small courts are boring. She tries to talk to Mona alone, but Mona said that they were late for the fitting. Hanna was about to follow Mona, when her purse spilled open. As she picks up her stuff, Hanna runs into an old guy friend from school named Lucas. Hanna tells him she has to go, but Lucas tells her that she has to pay the bill, which she does. Hanna gets a text from `A' telling her that one of her old friends was hiding something from her and that Mona wasn't her friend at all. Hanna is waiting for Mona at their lunch table to give her her birthday present. As she's waiting for Mona, Hanna goes through some old texts from Ali. Hanna's gift turns horribly wrong when the plane in the sky spelled, "Get ready to fart with Mona." instead of "Get ready to party with Mona." To top it all off, the two mean girls got a hip pop star to play at Mona's party. NOW, Hanna is officially uninvited to Mona's party. I know who did it, `A', which is Mona. Probably exactly how she wanted it to happen. Bitch. Everyone is talking about Hanna and how she's now a big loser. Poor Hanna! Looks like `A' was right, Mona isn't really her friend. After all, she is `A'. Hanna sees Mona and confronts her about the rumors about her going around. Mona says that she's only said true things about her, which means that she has been spreading rumors about her. Mona just called her a loser. Bitch. Hanna just thought of something interesting. Mona doesn't feel comfortable changing in front of others. I wonder why? Could she have had some surgery to make herself like Ali? Hanna is accidentally in the boy's bathroom and she's crying in front of Lucas, a loser of a guy! She is actually going to go somewhere with Lucas tomorrow as friends... Wow, I think Hanna might actually end up liking him more than a friend. (Then, her descent to loserdom would be complete.) YES! I KNOW IT! When Hanna saw Mona leaving the clinic that day is because she had lipo done there! But, why if Mona is `A' would she reveal something like that? Maybe Mona isn't `A'. Hanna is with Lucas at a park and she is wondering what kind of a surprise could Lucas be giving her. Hanna isn't sure whether she should spread the rumor about Mona around, because it felt different to do it to Mona, who was practically her best friend maybe even her only since Ali disappeared. YAY! The surprise is Hanna and Lucas are going to go up in a rainbow striped hot air balloon! Lucas told Hanna about his suicidal brother and how keeping busy keeps him sane, when Hanna admitted that she eats cheese-based snacks and then throws them up to keep sane! I cannot believe that she just told him that and neither can she! She just told Lucas about `A'! I wonder what's going to happen now!?! Hanna decided to tell Lucas everything and he still likes her. He says that he's glad that she isn't Alison, because he never liked her after she started a rumor about him in the 7th grade. Lucas says he likes her!!! She agrees to Lucas's invitation to his antiparty to Mona's birthday party. Hanna buys a new blackberry, because she refuses to think that everyone would drop her that fast because of the fight with Mona. For once, she wishes that `A' would text her. So, she buys a new phone thinking that her old one is broken. Mona walked into T-Mobil just as the guy behind the counter is having a hard time hooking her up with a new phone. Hanna tells Mona about the message on her garage roof and she apologizes to Mona about everything. Mona tells her that maybe things will go back to the way they were again and Hanna wonders what that maybe means. When Hanna goes home, her mom tell her that a package arrived for her. The package was her court dress to Mona's party. She thinks that must mean that she is re-invited. She decides to cancel her little party with Lucas. Hanna's court dress is really tight and I think Mona wanted it that way to make Hanna look really fat. Mona had changed the court dress on her and Mona was wearing the same dress as her. Mona told her that she changed the dress than she called Hanna a whale. `A' had sent Hanna the dress and she fell. The dress slit open and Mona and the court girls just let it happen and turned their back on her. Hanna tells the others about Mona's lipo, but no one seems to believe her. The big t.v. on screen showed everything that happened in replay. Lucas came to the rescue, but how did he know to come? Lucas gave Hanna a sweatshirt and a pair of shorts. She starts to cry in Lucas's car. She ended up on Lucas's lap and they started kissing. He ended up on top of her and touching her stomach and took his shirt off. They laid together like that for awhile, then someone was trying to come into the room. Hanna thought it was `A'. An old man ended up coming into the room. They left and Lucas walked her back to Mona's party to get her car and he said that he would call her tomorrow. As Hanna's driving away, she getts a text from `A' saying that she guessed that it wasn't lipo Mona got and to not believe everything that she hears. `A''s number wasn't unknown caller. It was an actual number and Hanna knows exactly who it is. Hanna waits in the parking lot for the other girls to arrive. She sees a strange car and then she sees Aria and Emily's car. She started running towards their car, when Aria shouted at her to look out. The SUV was going straight towards Hanna. Hanna was hit by the SUV!!!!!!!OMG!!!!

Current DLS:
Feels guilty about Toby's death/ being with Maya/Parents have forced her into the Tree Tops program/Rosewood Day's NEW It Girl/

Emily: Still feels really guilty for Toby's death. She feels like she was responsible for it. Her mom keeps on leaving her self-help books to help her deal with Toby's death. Her mom thought that Toby was her boyfriend, but he wasn't officially. Em is seeing Maya secretly and we all know that with `A' around a secret isn't a secret for long. Maya wants them to be more open about it, but Em wants to take it slow. Hears about the Rosewood Stalker, which is basically a peeping Tom. Someone is trying to gain information from people by peeping. Em has decided to tell the police about `A', even though `A' had told them not too. Her sister and fellow teammates are acting really weird at the swim meet. People in the stands were reading the heat sheet too interestingly. Two boys taunted Em and Mona personally invited her to her birthday party, which was very weird because none of these things ever happens to her. Everyone knows about the kiss between her and Maya. The photo is everywhere of them two kissing. Aria comes up to her telling her that they need to talk. `A' sent Em a text saying that since she was going to out `A', `A' was going to out her, which `A' did.

A: I think that Mona could be `A'. Don't ask why, but I think she could be. Ali teased her for years and so did the others. Why wouldn't she want revenge? Mona also always seems to be around, when things go wrong. When they were young, they spent time avoiding Mona. She was always walking across the street or trying to hang with them. I think stalking them and watching them from afar made her feel apart of their group. I think that she is most definitely `A'. I mean, why else would she be so cool with all of Hanna's problems and freak out when she finds out that Hanna has been keeping secrets from her. Especially since now, they are practically BFFs. The way Mona reacted to Hanna being with the others also adds to my suspicion. OMG! I think Mona was sending threatening messages to Ali, before she was murdered! Maybe, she even murdered Ali! Although a text from `A' says different, I think that she did kill Ali. But, just maybe, Mona isn't `A'. Last message from `A': She knew too much. - A
rows.title: Mona?

rows.text:
Hello,

Idiocracy largely based its wit and humor on a variety of comedy often referred to as "stupid humor" or "stupid comedy." I knew this before beginning this epic DVD journey. Although I'm not a fan of this type of humor, I was willing to sit through the many idiotic moments to explore the concept the film investigates.

The Plot: If you read the product description, the plot centers on a gradually decreasing human intelligence in the species that takes place over a 500 year period. Essentially, human existence in 2505 has devolved or been "dumbed down" by an overload of commercialization and mass media entertainment. In this new evolutionary era, the fate of the country (perhaps the world) rests on the shoulders of our intrepid protagonists, who hold the distinctive positions of Pentagon librarian and street prostitute in 2005 society. The fate of the species rests on these two to clean up humanity's mess after centuries of ignorance and decadent behavior.

The Concept: One of my favorite political philosophers told an allegory about a prehistoric society from an evolutionary biologist's point of view. To paraphrase him, "there are about 50 homo-sapiens huddled around a fire at night. The roar of a sabre-toothed tiger echoes in the distance. Scared, the group huddles closer to the fire for safety. Finally, 3 or 4 of the most brave, intelligent, and concerned men--stout men of character--decide to do something about the tiger threat. So they march off into the night with their spears, rocks, and little wooden shields. Come morning, only 2 of the merry band have returned, but they bring back the carcass of a mighty sabre-toothed tiger. So, the group has roasted tiger for supper that evening and all is well until...

...another roar in the distance. Once again, the group huddles closer to the fire with nervous eyes gazing beyond the fire's warm light and blinking hard at the thought of being eaten. Finally, our 2 valiant heroes from the last night decide to do something about the threat. They recruit 2 other tribesmen of lesser skill and sally to save the camp from danger. In the morning, 3 are dead and 1 is carrying the body of a slain sabre-toothed tiger. Meanwhile, as the 2-day sabre-toothed adventure takes place men of charm, deceit, and indulgence--blighted men skilled only in the use of their personality--take advantage of the situation by mating with the tribe's females thereby producing a lesser quality of offspring. And so on goes history, the best among the population are systematically replaced overtime with an inferior quality of human stock." I think the story is hilarious. :)

Fortunately, the political philosopher and I share the view that evolutionary biology, insofar as human beings are concerned, is a little off base. However, when you combine it with an influx of commercialization, mass media entertainment, and social indoctrination to consumption and attention pursuit, the idea of a "dumbed down" society looks less like a fantasy. Idiocracy gives a version of what this reality could look like.

In the movie, we have a mixture of bad breeding and escapism to fantasy that results in a society crushed under the weight of its own superficial excesses. It's barely able to function coherently because satisfaction of baser instincts (eating, sex, violence, and entertainment) has replaced higher-capacity thinking (reading, writing, intellectual discovery, and analysis). ***SPOILER ALERT*** So when the Idiocratic society discovers a smart ol' boy from the 21st century among them, they demand that he fix all of their problems. Simple enough, right? Especially, for a guy who actually thinks that the people of the 26th century invented a time machine that will return him to his own time. A time machine located at, excuse me, CostCo. ***END ALERT*** Hahahahahahahaaha!!! :)

The Comparison (WOW!! The parallelisms look like fiction becoming reality. If you don't believe, just take a look around next time you're at the mall):

Delusions: People are increasingly finding it difficult to distinguish fantasy from reality. Today, we have people who seriously believe that a boogeyman lurks in EVERY shadow, not just one, but EVERY shadow (criminals, sexual predators, terrorists, clowns (lol), the devil, etc.)--a fear propagated by our mentally-astute *cough*, wise, and all-knowing, EHEM!! (throat clears)...leaders. We've been indoctrinated through commercialization and mass media to fear our neighbors, not love them. ***AUTHOR'S NOTE*** If you hear the sound of upchuck, that's the sound of it depositing in a toilet along with any remaining warm feelings I have for most political/society leaders and so-called pop cultural gurus. Rrrrrraaaaaaaalllllllffffffff! Wheeeeezzzzee'ah!! There it goes again, 'scuse me. ***END NOTE***

There are some people who really think that the zombie apocalypse could begin as soon as next season's "The Walking Dead" is over. A buddy of mine read a story about a guy who chewed some ol' boy's face off in Florida. Then he revealed to me that he had cleaned all of his guns and stocked up on extra ammunition just so he could be prepared to survive the coming zombie wars. He was quite serious at the time. My many reassurances to him that it wasn't what he thought did little to alleviate his fears. "Whoa," I said to myself. It wasn't until a few other more level-headed individuals recommended that he give it some time to see if the apocalypse comes. He began to back off his fear, fortunately. Whew!! Close call.

***ANOTHER AUTHOR'S NOTE (on the Zombie Apocalypse)***I for one, believe the zombie apocalypse began in circa 1900, when people urbanized and celebrity worship began. Initially, it started with one or two people per city; usually, newspaper journalists. It gradually spread, zombies creating more zombies, until the early 1990s when the internet went commercial.***END NOTE***

The following is a fictional continuance for the above paragraph which ends with a reference to the movie "Idiocracy." Enjoy!

(Queue thematic music)

Zombie numbers exploded!! Now there are zombies everywhere! Today was no different than any other day. As I write this, a flood of memories make my brain spin. The kid who mows my grass is inseparable from his iPod--constantly "jacked" into mind-numbing minutia. I've never seen him without it.

(Shallow breathing)

Women I have dated who found it imperative to check every text message or Facebook alert, no matter how trivial, that comes into their phones while we're having dinner and in conversation. I've seen dorm room college students from my last university playing "World of Warcraft", "Halo", and "Call of Duty" for hours and sometimes days--skipping class and earning poor marks, then blaming it on anyone/thing, but themselves. People at work trading stupid youtube videos then watching them together with grunting whoops, hollars, and school-boy/girl giggles following!

(Eyes dialating)

A while back, I watched Dr. Phil counseling some poor young lass and her mom that Farmville isn't real. I've seen people glued to iPads with subscriptions to NetFlix or Hulu catching up on the latest drama-filled intrigue. I've driven by zombie lines outside theaters waiting for the midnight showings of Star Wars, Harry Potter, Twilight, Batman, and now, Hunger Games. Many zombies had dressed up like fictional characters. I couldn't help but notice the empty stares sometimes with mouths agape and back-and-forth swaying many of them displayed as they filtered into the theater doors.

(Skin going cold and color going pale at the thought. A sharp tingle travels down the length of my spine)

(Several hours later)

Folks, I am in a panic. Today, I shopped for groceries. I casually looked at the checkout stands and was barraged with Celebrity and Entertainment magazines. My feeling is these magazines sell out quickly to the zombie hordes. "Worship Kim Kardashian," one magazine seems to whisper. "Sexy abs in 30 days," one health rag bellows while another reads, "Buff, Bikini Body in Less than 2 Months." "Get the scoop on the latest fashion trends." "Check out Miley Cyrus's new 'do." "Anna Paquin's Baby Bump!" "Angelina Jolie says 'Back Off, He's Mine!' to Jennifer Aniston (over Brad Pitt)." A strange feeling is coming over me. Something is not right. It's like a nauseating infection that I NEED to get rid of. Perhaps, it'll pass after I have some Starbucks coffee. ;)

(Panicked breathing)

Finally home! I made it. I know what the feeling is from. The zombie-like ways are beginning to infect me. I'll relax by eating my 12" chicken cabanera for the low, low price of $8.99 at Quizno's. Crap! The "commercializers" are trying to infect me. There has to be a cure or a vaccine somewhere!! I see that there are more advertisements littering my mail stack and newspaper bin. Leaflets, fliers, brochures, pamphlets, and junk mail all telling me to buy, buy, buy. Here's a sampling: Credit card offers from JP Morgan/Chase and Citigroup gleefully announcing that they have pre-approved me to go into debt with them. Zit cream so I can stop `em before they sprout, then a girl might want to have sex with me. Motivational speaking seminar to bring out a more confident, secure me, then a girl might want to have sex with me. Shampoo, body wash, deodorant by Axe to make me smell like a man, then a girl WILL want to have sex with me. Damn it! I need to burn these! The zombies are getting close. They'll single me out, make fun of me `cause I'm not like them, and guilt me into consumerism, sexually objectify women, and pop culture. They can smell my resistance to these phenomena like flesh-eating zombies smell blood and brains. If I resist them, they will blog about me on their zombie Facebook. I need to go in hiding. I need to avoid bad things being said about me on zombie Facebook.

(Heart racing. I hear creaking floorboards and dragging feet!!)

Oh no! A girl friend texted and just asked to "friend" me on zombie Facebook. Thankfully, I don't have an account. Gasp! Someone just emailed me a link to a stupid youtube video featuring a man crashing his car into a lake. A junk mail message by a fake, sexy woman feigning interest in me is titled "Meow honey, wanna be my f*** buddy tonight?" it seems to purr. I know! I'll delete all of it. That'll keep me safe from the zombie infection that seems to be everywhere.
On my internet homepage, there's a big, bold advertisement to join Netflix among many others, which may expose me to zombie infection. I've got it, I'll change my homepage default settings. Yahoo! is advertising, "see who's viewed you" on my non-existent profile on a singles website. I'll fight back by disconnecting my internet, just as soon as I post this blog to warn the world! Maybe, just maybe there are uninfected people still left. I've got to warn those who are not infected! Maybe we can join together and strike off into the safety of the Alaskan forests. I've got to save humanity!

(Door knob twisting on the door to my barricaded room. Scratching on the door. I freeze. My breathing shudders. Queue suspense music)

Oh no folks, they are here. They found me!!! They won't get in for a while. I have my place zipped up tight. No TV, no radio, iPhone, iPad, HDVD, nothing. The bad news is, I hear them moaning outside my door. I think there is 5 or 6 of them. Their dialect is angled toward pop culture/consumerism; clear and present symptoms of infection.

One groans, "(zombie moan) Ohhh-whoooaaaahhhhhh, check out my new coin-flipping app on my iPhone, hee hee hee...ooooooaaaaahhhhhhhh!"

Another utters, "New season of Spartacus can be seen on Amazon.com for $2.99 an episode...ooooooaaaahhhhhhh...let's watch it from my HDVD on my, heh heh, flatscreen TV from Walmart that I went further into debt for...ahhh-whoooohhhhh!!!!"

A low growl voice says, "I got an invite on zombie Facebook for a party at Suzy-Beth the sorority girl's house. We can take beer there, and, ah ha ha ha, you know, hee hee, try to hook up. She's got some bangin' friends, yo...ooooowhhooaahhhhhhhhhh!!!!!!"

"Whoooowhhhooooaaaahh, hey, man, it's National Talk-Like-A-Pirate Day, 'yo hoe me matey,' heh hee heh...ooooooooooooo!!" another zombie gushes.

"Yo, bro, you're sista' is sooooo fine. So, like, WTF, can you, like, hook a brother up so I can tap that sweet...raahhhaaarrrrr!!" (zombie growl).

Incidentally, my sister is calling me on my outdated blackberry: "The Royal wedding was, like, soooooo amazing!!! I was, like, oh my God!! Her maid of honor was oh sooooooo totally pretty! Did you see it! Did you see it!! Hee hee hee (school girl giggles). Love you, big brother! Mu'ah! Whoooooooaaaaaaaaaaaaahhhh oh oh oh oohh!!" Click!

Not much time left, folks. They're coming. My house is surrounded and I can't call in the Marines, they may be infected too. I see the door is losing strength at the hinges. My choices are clear. I can load my own guns and fight it out with the zombies or take my own life. If I take my life, I won't be able to save the planet like that ol' boy did in "Scott Pilgrim vs. the World." I won't be able to "friend" all of my future children that I will be unable to properly provide for because I'm in debt, working two jobs, going through my third divorce, and paying alimony/child support. I must stay alive because then I can get those new kickin' dockers my friends all have and complement my wardrobe with Joop cologne--it's a fashion necessity. Not to mention, there's an entertainment article explaining all of the cool cars that celebrities drive. Speaking of which, I have to know if Katie Holmes makes it. She's been through so much since her divorce and Tom was so hard on her during the marriage. I hope she finds someone special. Like true love. Like all those wonderful conversation lines that couples say on TV to make the relationship special. I see that soooo much in real life, and it makes me feel, like, everything is going to be totally okay. ;) I know, my next blog should be about this! Tee hee!!

My "friends" are in 'da hizz'ous. What was I thinking!! They're wondering why my door was barricaded. "I don't know?" I said. "Strangely, I really can't remember anything beyond a few minutes ago." Oh well! I show off my collection of newly-acquired baubles that serve no purpose, but look cool. "Oooo aaaahhhh!" My friends muse. Strange how familiar that sounds. Okay folks, my friends and I are going order pizza from Pizza Hut and watch a "1,001 Ways to Die" on television. I hear it's, like, 'da bomb, man. Heh heh heh!! Hold on, folks. Something is coming up from my throat, "OHHHH--WHOOOOOOOAAAAAAAAAHHHHHHHH!!!!!" (zombie moan)

Conclusion:

WELCOME to the Idiocratic States of America (ISA), 2012. Guess the movie has some truth to it. ;)

FINAL NOTE: This is meant to review the movie without too many spoilers and also express the author's views of it as well as how it relates to our community. If the reader didn't notice, the author likes to entwine a little humor in the writing. Comments are always welcome, preferably ones without insult. However, if the one commenting feels like an insult is warranted, feel free. The author promises not to take insults seriously. :) :)
rows.title: Idiocracy: An Interesting Concept

rows.text: The writing of this book Fatal Vision took so much research I'm sure . But the writing seemed to write with such ease its as if he were right their taking notes for all the contents in the book !! Including the murders . As a little girl I saw the movie and was aware of what happened in the Sharon Tate murders as well . Even as a little girl I thought about Sharon and her murder , with her being pregnant , as something that shocked me ! Jeffery Macdonalds murders the same thing . I was aware of this book as a little girl - the murders even though they hapoened before I was born - but they continued to be talked about afterwards for a decade and of course on and on - thats how I was ably to see the movie . My sister and I were very aware type kids . She was born in 1971 but still these murders as Sharon Tate and the murders in her house never stopped being talked about !! The man that plays Jeffrey Macdonald does too great a job :( !! He plays this person too good actually and as a little girl I hated him ! Yes because I didn't believe he did it  just because the scenario fit - I believed then with my whole heart with how the story was presented that he did kill his family ! And I still do !! Especially after reading THIS book !! This book is so objectively written but so intricate the person writing it was just possessed to piece it as he went along PERFECTLY !!! He gave me the same view of this Jeffery Macdonald as I saw in the movie only worse and more intricate !! It's not because Collette is a woman and I'm a woman that I believe he did it either . But this book lets you see and hear play for play what was really important to Jeffery Macdonald . It was not his wife and kids that's for sure . And the way he LIES !! I mean he seriously should have thought that lie out more . But he chose a night and new he had to kill his family whether an argument ensued or not ! He knew it was now or never . He never wanted kids or that kind of lifestyle . He wanted a flashy life . Although he was capable of telling you how both his children's personalities were - Kimberly being " soft and feminine " kristy being two years old and yet she was a little " toughy " sticking up for her " big " sister when neighborhood kids would tease her . Still my father can recite things about my sister and I the same way it does not mean that their endearment their . It doesn't mean they wouldn't kill you or pay child support .
