In this case he said all these endearing things that he remembered but it was not enough to stop the murder and my father didnt pay child support yet he was an excellent father when I was very little then he disappeared . So that's a bunch of crap ! It baffles me that now that we have the Internet their are people that feel they can do a better job at deciding that this man didnt kill his family !! His personality and how he views people especially women in his life that he was intimate with show a man with a set mind on certain things that pertain to his life . Dependent is one . That word sort of turned him on it seems ! He did these horrific murders . Wanted Collete's family support and thank GOD they caught on !!!! But he really did not want their support he was just scared what they would do if it came out in their lifetime while they had any physical strength left themselves !! Why would he think they would stop grieving their daughter and grandchildren ?? When people forgive in an instance where their children have been murdered they don't always forgive the way some idiots do by baking cookies for the mirderer ! Sharon Tate's family all died from cancer because of how this ate away at their very core souls ! They NEVER got over her death !!! And the reason is because a lot of times the personality of the person killed was too good for this world but still WE are not GOD to make the decision to take them off this universe ! GOD even knows we need good examples down here on this universe !!! The way Collettes personality was described was that of a good kind person . She could have been stubborn in a sweet way too . But for Jeffrey Macdonald to underestimate his family is beyond me . Or any story similar to this one . But this one especially . He could have abruptly left Collette and said I don't want to be married or have kids anymore . That is that simple . He underestimated that they would not have been able to move on ! His children would have moved on . In speaking of Collette coming from a family where her own father commited suicide I still don't believe she would have done the same thing . At least I hope she would not . His oldest daughter Kimberly who he said was so soft and feminine would have rose to the occasion many times in her life had she been allowed the chance to ! I mean she was an Aries !! He over kilked kristy , the baby  because she was so tough even as a two year old that she put her little hands up to fight the killer blows of her assailant - her father . I'm sure she made the most noise ! He lied so bad in telling his story that I would have just broke down an admitted to the murders ! He said their were four people in front of him ??? But could hear his wife saying " oh Jeff why are they doing this to me !! What she was saying is why are you doing this to me ! She watched with her HEART the man she LOVED the father of her children kill her and her babies !!! It's like the first time the knife struck near Sharon Tate's stomache how she must have felt when she knew her killers didnt care a out her or her baby she screamed " oh noooooo ' because the baby she was two weeks before having was about to die ! Same for Collette ! In her instance not just the baby in her stomache but she saw with her eyes that he was capable of killing their children and she was a witness to that - that this mean meant business - his business . His life was more important . To underestimate his family ??? To underestimate what they meant to her family !!! Her step father !!! Her mother who was heartbroken to the very end !! Not every person who has to forgive bakes cookies for the murderers of their families !!!!! Not everyone that has a loved one murdered marries the murderer !!! They are infected with these murders for life !!! Infected !!! Collette was a Taurus she would have found another beautiful man and she would have got tough . She would have moved on and especially under the careful watch of her loving family!!! Kimberly would have moved on and she would have been a toughy herself . She may have the DNA of her moms traits genetics of her soft feminine side but she would have come into her own and she would have defended he little sister herself !!! Kristy would have been able to defend her mom and her sister in the future if he came around harassing them about clearing up his image for walking out ! Had he at least just let them live and just say I don't want to live this life I want to live a flashy life . To underestimate Collette , Kimberly , Kristin ??? It's beyond me - they would have MOVED ON !!! He thought he was too wonderful for them to move on . Now we have people who actually believe that maybe he didnt kill his family ??? Now that we are able to do what I'm doing now , posting how I feel ???  He killed them !! All three ! When u look at the crime scene photos ??? Oh my gosh !!! Her little hand is lifted up a bit as if she tried to get up ????  I cried watching the movie mostly because he could do this to them under no circumstance ? He just knew he had to do it and the first blow sealed the deal !! This isn't just about the physical murders . No it's about Jeffery Macdonalds mind . How he talks and what he talks about and how he explains situations about  his life, his persona is very transparent . It's seems Mr. McGinnis the writer leaves  all the evidence of his persona and we take it from there . Depending on the sickness of our own minds we can tell what is truth here . My sister read this book when she was 14 and said it was great ! I read it now as an adult and she said you are going to like that book because its a page turner . We also as I said saw the movie . We sneaked and saw it on TV . When someone asked me what part are you reading of the book now I say I'm reading the part where this doctor husband is lying . I despise his lie the most . How he lies and how even the most unaffected person can see he lied . I never give the best reviews . I prefer to debate in person because I always win . I also never follow . But I have too too much to say when it comes to this story because I sympathize with Collette , her children , her mother , her step father , her brother . Collete was 26 years young ? Her children were still babies . They would have moved on from this man . I know it .
rows.title: This book was an A ➕➕➕!!

rows.text:
After finishing Stephanie Ahn's debut and Book 1 of her Harrietta Lee Series, "Deadline," literally in a blink of an eye (novella length only), that left me wanting more (Harry's ongoing adventures plus a certain demon!), I had to read Book 2 right away! Plus, I was dead curious about Ahn's sophomore writing and storytelling skills. Needless to say, I devoured it at top speed and came out of it totally and completely entertained, satiated, satisfied, and more inspired and thrilled than ever! Ahn is most definitely a talented writer and tale-spinner of the wildest, most daring, uninhibited and imaginative kind in the world of urban fantasy! 

"Bloodbath." Yes, that's the word Ahn used to title the 2nd installment of her Harrietta Lee series. And yes, it means what it means, literally and figuratively, and she made no qualms about it, whatsoever, in bringing that title to life in weaving the sequel to Harry's adventures as a disgraced blood witch earning her keep as an "unofficial" P.I. in the streets of New York! Ahn definitely nailed the "sequel" formula perfectly, imo: BIGGER. BETTER. MORE. Much, much more.

The first thing I noticed when I started the book was, it read very differently than Book 1. Whilst the format of Ahn's narrative remained the same - real-time first-person - the style was decidedly more structured and controlled, the rhythm and flow more settled, with a darker and more serious atmosphere, stakes were higher because the plot was much more intricate with complex implications and more far-reaching effects. Another thing I noticed was the setting/set up. As I read on, there was a distinct flavour of a Harry Potter/Fantastic Beasts influence specifically in the Ministry of Magic/Magical Congress of the USA realm and all it represented in the magical world. For Ahn's Harry and her world, it was called the "Council" which, like MoM/MACUSA, governed every magical creature living amongst the human world. In this case, it's more like MACUSA since Harry's world is in America. The organisational structure was set up just like any multi-national company, with the International Council as the parent company, with regional councils governing assigned areas. The featured Council in Harry's world (New York) was called the Northeastern American Regional Council. Wicked, innit? Can you imagine the potential for story expansion, what with the existence of other regional councils, inside and outside of America, and the International Council headed by this neutral judge and tie-breaker called the Arbiter? I simply LOVE it! 

Now with the existence of a "government" (the Council) in the mage (magical) world, obviously there would be politics, power play, power struggle, rules governing the mages (magic folk) in conjunction with the non-mage (ala "No-Mag" in FB, eh?) world. In this installment, Ahn tossed us right into the Council's world. She had given us a brief intro about the NARC (gosh, doesn't cast a positive light on the Council with THAT acronym, does it? Bloody cheek, Ms. Ahn!) in Book 1, but in this sequel, she opened the door a bit wider allowing us to take a closer look at the Council - the who, the what, the how - which thrilled me to bits because ever since Book 1, I really wanted to discover more about the "all-powerful" entity. Albeit Ahn didn't reveal a whole lot but enough to whet my appetite and hope for more to come in future installments! 

This sequel picked up a week after the end of "Deadline." What started off as a simple missing persons case for Harry, turned out to be much more than that when her BFF, Joy, was discovered missing and reported by Joy's client-turned-close mate, Nikki. From then on, everything just spiralled out of control, the deeper Harry delved into in her investigation. I won't divulge anything else about the plot because it needs to be explored directly by the reader in order to feel its significance, implications and effects. All I can say is, Ahn cleverly weaved a simple web that quickly mutated into something bigger, darker, wilder, with twists and turns that kept me on my toes and my adrenaline level pumping right until the end with her deliberate pacing from (relatively) calm waters in the first act, to rising waves in the second, that broke into a full-blown tsunami in the final one! 

With a bigger more intricate plot meant more characters, obvs. Speaking of, I was dead chuffed anticipating what Ahn had in-store for my favourite character, Lilith, the adorable, mischievous and mysterious demon, in this installment! And I was bowled over with excitement that Lilith truly put a solid stamp in Harry's world in this story. Forget about her relationship with Harry for a mo, Lilith's back story was still largely shrouded in mystery even though Ahn revealed a little more about her in the context of her "contract" with Harry. But Ahn's very subtle description and portrayal of Lilith throughout this book were very nuanced that carried a deeper meaning, albeit unspoken. Whether it was a deliberate move by Ahn to create a deeper layer of mystery and intrigue with Lilith's character via a series of richly expressed non-verbal body language, I thought it was brilliantly executed. Lilith was already an enigma since Book 1. With added nuances simmering beneath her character in this story, I'm even more curious about Lilith's true character and her purpose beyond the confines of her relationship with Harry. I'd love to explore her back story especially after a certain reveal toward the end of the book. Now, back to her relationship with Harry, I utterly LOVED Ahn's depiction of their chemistry together, this implicit bond they had (ever since Book 1, really!) - Lilith's dominant behaviour to Harry's submissive - was utterly captivating, borderline intoxicating to witness, what with that undeniable magnetic pull of connecting energy like an invisible thread that was binding them closer together at each passing time, much to their exasperation, which interestingly, the reluctance came more from Lilith's end than Harry's. Who was Lilith, really? What was her purpose with Harry? Why did she seem more unsettled and perplexed every time she felt something? Why that secrecy in her role as a demon in the demon world? So many questions. So much intrigue. Maybe I'm just reaching but I have a feeling that as Ahn expands the series, Lilith's role will slowly reveal its true purpose. Well, that's my wishful thinking, anyway! In any case, Ahn has created a richly layered, deeply empathetic character in a demon that I'm completely enamoured of!

Apart from Lilith returning as part of the cast, Luce, Harry's coven sister, was back, too, albeit briefly but the significance of her role as a healer was defined and delved into a little more in this story. I do hope to find out more about her in future installments. As for new characters, I loved every one of them, particularly Nikki, a client-turned-good friend of Joy's, who quickly bonded with Harry in the wake of Joy's disappearance. Just when I thought Ahn had created an intriguing character in Nikki, she pulled a rabbit out of the hat about this unassuming character that I was simply gobsmacked! Bloody brilliant is all I'm gonna say about it! You simply have to read it to find out! And then, there was Harry's computer/tech geek of a close mate, Izzy (Isabella)!! Another fantastic character from Ahn's brilliant mind! Two words came to mind when I explored these two characters: Unique. Diverse. In every sense. 'Nuff said. And then there were others, namely one of the Council Enforcers whose name I won't disclose because the reveal was funny, who had amusing chemistry with our adorably idiotic Harry! Then Dick...omg, what a perfect name for him because he was a bloody idiot of a knob, and then Junhyun, the son of Harry's neighbour....Ooo... All I can say is, Ahn is a bloody creative storyteller! Yeh, the outside-the-box thinking kind! Well done, Ms. Ahn! Even the antagonists were utterly intriguing to me who were weaved into the story ever-so brilliantly. Even that true muppet of a vamp! Blimey! Never read a vamp with THAT kind of nutter juice in them! Fan-bloody-tastic! There was one character, the Arbiter, who immediately piqued my curiosity because of a certain reveal which I'm hoping is a breadcrumb that Ahn deliberately threw in this story for future references. Hmm.... 

As for Harry, I loved how Ahn shaped her character so far. I really enjoyed exploring her dilemmas, her struggle to block out her emotional feelings, whether it was about her mentor, her family, her missing friend or more crucially, about Lilith, all of which Ahn had deftly depicted in Harry's character development, with resonating effect. Harry's ongoing struggle with her self-worth (her constant self-deprecation, for starters!) that Ahn ever-so subtly injected into Harry's character as she continued with her journey of loss, contemplation, guilt, as she tried to reconcile, let go, accept and hopefully later on, embrace love. There was no romance in this book, just like the previous installment, but subtle nuances were there. That's what I love about Harry and Lilith's relationship. There was nothing deeply concrete yet but there was certainly a deliberate slow-burn of sorts in terms of how these two polar opposites, yet, somehow shared an affinity, viewed their relationship. Their interactions that were mostly superficial with loads of teasing and sarcasm and dry wit usually from Lilith's end with defensive and funny reactions from Harry, which always fell flat, much to her dismay, that only invigorated Lilith's gleefully playful condescension! For e.g....

Lilith stands up on her tiptoes—or rather, stands up even higher on her toes, since she’s got those fancy digitigrade cat feet—and pats the top of my head. “It’s okay, Witchy, it’s not your fault you’re stupid,” she consoles in a voice that’s entirely too charitable for her smug face.

GUFFAW! 

But at certain moments, with Lilith's abrupt change of behaviour when she thought things were on the edge of being a little serious between them, they would then turned quietly contemplative but laced with emotional resonance. Also, the fact that Lilith was a demon so she had to adhere to some strict rules within the demon world provided another layer of complexity as far as they were concerned. To see how Lilith manoeuvre her way around them when it came to Harry was utterly enthralling. This relationship between Harry and Lilith, is one that must only be tackled using the slow-burn approach, which Ahn had skilfully weaved the layers that were then slowly peeled off little by little at every stage, with intoxicating flair. Obviously, there are still many hidden layers left unpeeled, if what was included in this installment is any indication. I do hope it spans the entire series, how ever many installments Ahn is coming up with. At this point, after reading 2 installments, I can see at least 5 to 7 books in total because of how Ahn has laid out the canvas depicting Harry's world. So many possibilities, so much potential, because she's opened up many angles already in the form of the Council and the others including the International Council, the Arbiter, Harry's family past, Lilith, Luce's story, the demon world, even other possibilities, i.e. Izzy and Nikki, other connected magical creatures, the possibilities are just endless at this point, really. Just like J.K. Rowling's Fantastic Beasts Series. Which makes me wonder if Ahn, like Rowling, has the entire Harrietta Lee series planned out in a certain number of installments already. Christ, I do hope so! Makes my level of anticipation even more acute! 

Back to the plot, without divulging anything besides the aforementioned, let's just say that whilst I was devouring every bit of adventure, the thrills, those twists and turns, the suspense, and the action, there were moments that reminded me of, amongst other things, Harry Potter's pensieve sequence, and *gasp* Stephen King's "Doctor Sleep," which, speaking of, I had JUST finished reading before I delved into this book! So imagine my utter aghast at a certain direction of the plotline where I was like, "Whatthebloodyhell?!" The parallel was just uncanny especially right after reading one and then the other. I'm sure if I hadn't read King's book right before this one, I wouldn't have freaked out like I did! Yup, what are the odds, eh? But there you go. And oh, the descriptions of the violent action sequences, the action moves, Harry's "OMG, NO!" impossibly harrowing "physical exertions" (alternative mild definition used here, mind!), and desperate predicament(s) were so vividly detailed I could feel every sensation! And Lilith! Oh, Lilith!!! As if I couldn't loved her character more, Ahn splayed even more honest-to-goodness angel dust of preciousness into this endearing, heart-tugging demon that I just...can't..!!! Ohhhhh!!!! Talk about an oxymoronic character, eh? Simply MARVELLOUS!!! Bloody well, done, Ms. Ahn! I bow to you, oh great one! I want MORE Lilith, please!!! Oops! Pardon me! Got a bit mad there for a mo! Needless to say, all the more reason to read this book is all I have to say about it. 

There were two memorable moments that absolutely struck me emotionally. One was the unbelievably suspenseful sequence in the final act that involved Lilith's heartbreaking anguish and her desperate moves, and later on, Harry's quieter, more contemplative action related to Lilith.... I can't say more because that scene needs to be read in order to capture that seemingly nonchalant but tethered with so much emotion that Harry herself couldn't quite comprehend but we as readers would feel. And that's why their relationship needs to be at a slow-burning pace. Let it simmer, let the flame grow leisurely throughout the passage of time because the reward at the end of it would be infinitely more enduring and memorable, you know, like Ron and Hermione, than otherwise depicted.

Then, there was the quiet, sombre and deeply affecting moment in the bath tub scene. The unspoken, the silence between Harry and Lilith with eyes that spoke volumes in their magnetic pull toward each other. They say that the eyes are a window to the soul. No truer words to describe what Harry and Lilith shared there and then, in that moment frozen in time. That heavy air of a certain feeling and emotion between them seemed to scare Lilith, which Ahn depicted with such subtlety but packed with emotional resonance! At least for me! I reckon when you get to that scene, you'll know what I'm getting at. 

There were several interactions especially toward the end between Lilith and Harry that only further deepened my utter adoration for them as mates, partners-in-crime, and hopefully something more. The one that really stuck was this exchange (only a snippet because...spoilers):

[Lilith] “Witchy, when did you forget that I’m a demon? ...I told you before. I’m a demon, not your personal Supergirl. I’m in this to protect my own interests. Don’t count on me to do anything else.”

[Harry] Normally, she’d say this kind of thing with an airy nonchalance. Right now, her voice is just hollow. Her body is tense against my back, and I’m haunted by shadows of the words she’s not saying. Or is that just the famous Hellish manipulation? She sounds genuine, but… I can’t be sure. You can never be sure with a demon.

The reprise of Lilith's "I'm a demon, not your personal Supergirl" reverberated throughout the book with affecting fervour. At least for me. 

All in all, I absolutely, positively LOVED this installment! Ahn has truly created an incredible series, with only 2 books in, deliberately dropping breadcrumbs at strategic points in every installment, effectively laying out her own "yellow brick road" for Harry & Co. and us, readers, to tread on and explore. The subtle breadcrumbs mean there's a larger plot at play in Harry's universe. There's just so much potential to explore so I hope Ahn has planned out the entire series with a blazing end - like how Rowling has mapped out her entire Fantastic Beasts Series. I loved the fact that Rowling announced firmly that the story would span 5 books, much like Harry Potter with 7 books, that's it. That's what I love to see in serials. A fantastically weaved saga with a finite end. I'm hoping Ahn's going the Rowling direction. *crossing fingers in anticipation*

If you haven't realised my view about this book already, "Bloodbath" is a RESOUNDING MUST-READ! An ENTHUSIASTIC Recommendation from me! And, please, if you haven't read Book 1, "Deadline," yet, I IMPLORE you to do so before you read this one. You'll not regret it especially if you're a fan of witches and demons, ghosts and psychics, fae and the likes, Harry Potter and Fantastic Beasts, ala the urban fantasy genre in either general (mainstream) fiction or lesbian fiction! But what makes Ahn's "Harrietta Lee Series" special is that it's Lesbian Fiction/LGBT Fiction with a multitude of diverse characters, be it race, size, physicality, sexuality or the likes, being represented. How wicked is that?! Simply marvellous!

With a knowledge that Book 3, "Flytrap" is coming out in 2020/21, I'm utterly chuffed about it! Psyching myself in anticipation, tbh!! Books 1 and 2 are definitely on my list of reread material! Ahn is undoubtedly on my list of newly discovered authors to watch now and will be looking out for all her future lesfic writing endeavours, that's for sure! What a young talent of a writer, indeed!

Well done, Ms. Ahn, and thank you for this rich, diverse, exciting, fun, always hilarious, compelling, utterly addictive series which I hope will end in all its glory with a few books to its collection that I will cherish like I do with HP, only this time, it's lesfic! Wicked! And... for gifting me Lilith, the infinitely cheeky, mischievous and totally endearing demon with a heart of gold! I hope she'll endure 'til the end with Harry & Co.! Cheers!
rows.title: A MUST-READ Book 2 Of The Already Addictive Series that's BIGGER. BETTER. MORE. Much, much more!

rows.text:
CHANGING COURSE-
''5 Life Changing Stars!!''

If you want a guy meets girl falls in love, gets married, has kids and lives happily ever after story.. This is NOT that story. When I 1st read the synopsis to the story I knew I just had to read it. I had a feeling it was going to be a different story then what I was use too. But, I wasn't prepared for what I was getting myself into. I can not begin to tell you how good this story is. There is so much different emotions going on in this. One minute I'm dying with laughter, the next I was crying.. Well, more like balling my eyes out.

Brett & Sarah meet at a bar. They fall head over heal in love with each other. Their best friends Caleb & Manda also fall in love with each other. Brett & Sarah get married. Thing's are great. Brett & Caleb are police officers. When they aren't working. The 4 of them are out hanging out and enjoying their life to the fullest. Until, the night that changed everything.

4 years later Brett & Caleb are still trying to pick up the pieces of their lives...

That's when we meet Jesse. She works at the coffee shop where the guys go and get their breakfast every morning. Jesse has such a crush on Brett. Every morning they flirt with each other like crazy. It's really cute to read. She finally gets the guts up to ask him out. Before he has the chance to say No. Caleb answers for him & he's forced to go.

When they have their 1st date Brett dropped a bomb that Jesse AND even me was not expecting. He was married!!! Except, not in the way we'd all think. Sara isn't the same woman you'd remember. She went from the happy go-lucky woman. Who's madly in love with Brett, to a shell of herself. Sarah hates Brett with everything she has. She hates herself even more. That night replays in her 24/7 & all she wants is it to end. Sarah wants Brett gone. Out of her life for good. Brett on the other hand, took his vows to heart, "In sickness & health. Till death do us part."

Jesse was shocked. When Brett saw her get upset it did something to him. It was then he realized he needed her in his life. They attempt the whole 'friend' thing. But, they both realize rather quickly that their friendship is a lot stronger. The feelings that they have for each other are not what they expected. Yet, every time they're together they ignore the tall, beautiful, blonde, crazy elephant in the room.

Brett realizes very quickly that Sarah wasn't the only one that died that terrible night 4yrs ago. They all did!! Except when he's around Jesse. He feels alive. He has starting to feel things that he hasn't felt since Sarah. Jesse knows that she is falling head over heals for Brett. But, knows that she must keep her heart guarded. Because, at the end of the day Jesse doesn't know if Brett can ever move on from Sarah. She dosen't just want to sit around and wait forever. Brett knows he must make a decision."

This is most definitely not your typical love triangle. Brett & Sarah are only married on paper. Brett & Jesse are falling harder & harder. Sarah is now on a mission. Make Jesse's life as miserable as possible. Caleb is the good guy. He wants Jesse to be safe. Wants Brett to move on. Wants Sara gone. When thing's take a turn for the worse. Brett must decide what & who he wants in his life. He needs to do what's best for everyone involved. That includes letting 1 of the 2 women he's ever loved go.

You will not be disappointed. For a debut novel Aly Martinez nailed it. I am really, really looking forward to read what she writes in the future!

STOLEN COURSE-
''5 Huge Heart Breaking Stars!!''

Aly Martinez has done it to me AGAIN!!! I was an absolute wreck from beginning to end of the book. Not that I was shocked by this. In her first book Changing Course we learned the 2 couples lives were changed forever! Brett & Caleb are police officers.Brett's wife Sarah & Caleb's fiance Manda were involved in a car accident. Sarah survived & Manda did not. We saw in Changing Course Brett moved on with Jesse. At the end of Changing Course Sarah went after Jesse. We ended it with Caleb carrying a broken Sarah out. It was there I knew I was in for it in Stolen Course. But, I was totally not prepared for what I got!!

We pick up exactly where we left off. Emma Jane Erickson who owns a photography business. Gets a call from her brother-in-law Brett. Not that it's out of the blue. He has been keeping her up to date on Sarah's life the past 5yrs. Emma is Sarah's little sister. Sarah has pushed everyone away including her sister. Now that Sarah has gotten herself into serious trouble Emma is on the 1st flight to her.

Caleb hates Sarah with everything he has. She killed the love of his life. He has spent the past 5yrs of his life doing the same thing. Working, going to the gym, having one night stand after the other, doing his woodwork in his garage. But, that's not the 2 most important things. He visits Manda's grave every day and every night he takes out the box in his closet. It has everything from that night. Caleb will do everything it takes to prove
Sarah killed Manda.

Caleb broke my heart or I should say he shattered my heart into pieces. He's literally broken inside. Nothing helps him. I honestly don't think anything will heal him. Not even getting Sarah thrown in jail.

Caleb, Brett & Jesse are at the hospital waiting to hear news about Sarah. Then, walks Emma and Caleb's world is instantly turned upside down!! He's instantly attracted to her. Emma quickly makes the decision to move back & help Sarah. Caleb promises her he'd let her know if anything comes up with Sarah before she gets back.

Emma is home & she can't stop thinking about Caleb. Caleb is in the same boat. He can't stop thinking about Emma. Which is not Caleb!! Well, they start talking on the phone all the time. They totally have an instant connection. Which I really, really enjoyed. It was nice seeing Caleb smile & laugh. And, well enjoying life. He was totally not used to what was happening. It had been 5 long years since he felt anything but hatred!

Caleb picks Emma up at the airport. These 2 aren't out to the truck yet & already have World War 3. Why... Easy Sarah!! They realize rather quickly that Sarah is a VERY VERY sore subject. They agree to never talk about her. Which is hard for Emma. I couldn't imagine being in her shoes.

Brett & Jesse still had a good part in this book. Which I loved!! I love how Jesse & Emma became quick friends considering Emma's sister wanting to her Jesse. I loved when the 4 of them were together. Then, add in Kara and OMG you'll be peeing your pants!! I loved how Kara & Jesse got Emma up to speed on the "vegetable report" which by the way is absolutely hilarious!!! But, it was nice that even though Caleb was not there for her when it came to Sarah at all. I mean you can't mention her name without s*** hitting the fan. Emma still had Jesse & Kara. Which was really awesome to see!!

Emma & Caleb are made for each other. Emma knows Caleb has a past and she accepts it. She knows he goes to Manda's grave everyday. She knows most of his body is tattooed in a memorial for her. Caleb;s heart is Manda's & Emma knows it. How she deals with it. The hell if I know!! But, Emma is awesome. Plain & simple.

Emma goes & sees Sarah weekly. Which is awesome because we learn exactly what is going on with Sarah. We find out why she pushed everyone away. There is literally so much we learn from the horrible night from everyone involved that my head literally spun & I gasped a lot.

Caleb & Emma get serious pretty quick. To the point that Caleb starts changing. And, in a good way!! Or so you think!! Emma accidentally finds the box. When she confronts Caleb he admits to still trying to find out the truth & still plans on taking Sarah down!

Emma leaves and Caleb is all alone. Again!! Who does he blame? Yup,you guessed it... Sarah.

Of course it doesn't end there. But, I will say A LOT, A LOT, A LOT, A LOT of stuff comes out after that. Good. Bad. Ugly. It all came out!!

Caleb also has to figure out what he wants. In order for him to be happy he needs to let a lot go. But, can he? Emma has to decide a lot of her own stuff out. What to do with Sarah. Can she forgive Caleb for how hes acted towards her sister. For not being there for her.

Aly hit a grand slam with this book!! She made you get attached to the characters. It didn't matter if they laughed, cried, got mad, smiled or were confused. Because whatever they did. Guess what... I did too!! Thanks for that Aly! I for one can not wait to see what happens next. I will tell you this. I know this book is considered a standalone. But, you totally must read Changing Course first!!

AMONG THE ECHOES-
''5 Heart Wrenching Stars!!''

One day she was Erica, the next she was Riley. Forced to hide. To pretend. Constantly afraid. Always wondering if she'll be found. Never opening herself up to anyone. Protecting herself and everyone else.

Wow!! Aly Martinez has done it to me again!! I have loved every book she has written. Every book is better then the last. Among The Echoes was insanely awesome. It is emotional like her Wrecked and Ruined series. Except on a completely different level and for a completely different reason. There are a few authors who can write a book and I don't have to read the synopsis, because it doesn't matter to me. I say this because I know that because their name is on the cover it won't matter what the book is about, I'll love it regardless. Aly Martinez is one of those authors for me!!

Erica Hill is in a situation that NOBODY wants to be in. And, I mean NOBODY!! Just when she thinks she is going to die. Leo saves her life.

3 years later there is no more Erica Hill. Today it's Riley Peterson and her cousin Dave. Riley is a shell of who she used to be. Riley is afraid of every sudden noise, unexpected knocks on a door. Don't touch her. She doesn't sleep. The ONLY person she trusts is Dave.

Dave has it made it his life mission to protect & save Riley. Dave can not get over what happened to Riley. Deep down he wishes he could get her to smile. To get her to become the person she was before that horrible experience she went through. The experience he had to witness. He never thinks he'll be able to help her. That is until they got a new neighbor.

Slate Adam Andrews is the World Heavyweight Champion of the world. Slate loves fighting in the ring. But, he HATES everything that comes with it. He doesn't like the cameras, the paparazzi, the fans touching him. He stays secluded. When he finishes a fight he goes home to the only place he can be alone. The place that he can escape. Ohio.

Slate runs into Riley. I mean literally runs into Riley. They both freak. Slate thinks she will recognize him. Riley thinks that since Dave is out of town for a few days that Slate is there to kill her. Slate can tell right of the bat Riley doesn't know who he is. But still introduces himself as Adam. He can tell she's broken. Slate (Now Adam) instantly wants to protect Riley.

Adam is the first guy Riley has noticed in 3yrs. That night Riley gets scared and runs right to Adam for help. Dave comes home to find Riley & Adam sleeping oh the couch. it doesn't end too pretty. Dave knows instantly who Adam really is. Agrees to keep it a secret. Adam doesn't believe Riley & Dave's story. But, agrees to let them keep it.

Over the next few weeks Riley & Slate get closer and closer. Slate knows that Riley isn't telling him the truth. He begs her to tell him. But, he never pushes her. Which is seriously so damn sexy!! Riley has felt alive for the first time in a long time. She has always felt safe with Dave and nobody else. Now, she has Adam and feels safe. But, she is even more scared then before. She knows if they find her and Dave then Adam's life is in danger as well.

Dave keeps insisting that it'll workout. Dave desperately wants Riley to start living. Seeing her smile and laugh means the world to him. I loved their relationship. They understand exactly what the other one is fighting in themselves. They're each others only family. One minute they are going back & forth like brother and sister. The next Dave is putting away his "Man Card" to be Riley's girlfriend. Which is hysterical btw. No matter how often they fight. They would die for the other one.

One night Riley & Adam are talking. He decides it's time to tell her the truth. Well, he knew Riley was going to be mad. But, he wasn't expecting what he got. Riley realizes if she gets found Slate will die. She won't do it. Riley breaks up with him.

Slate knows it's time for a lifestyle change. After spending time in LA to change what he knows deep down he should of done awhile ago.

Slate comes back to Ohio. Back to Riley. He tells her he needs to know the truth about her and Dave. Dave talks Riley into telling Slate EVERYTHING!!!

Slate makes a point to letting Riley know he's not leaving. Dave and Slate make it their live to protect Riley.How?? Riley and Dave back their lives. Erica, Leo & Slate start new. Together!! Or is it??

There was so many times I couldn't believe what I was reading. Slate/Adam & Dave/Leo are the hottest, sexiest guys. Who love Riley and would do anything to protect her. To see her smile. It was really nice to see. Dave & Riley are so broken. They never recovered. I didn't think they ever would. Until Slate entered!!

There was times I'd be reading and find myself cheesing. Other times I'd find myself jumping if there was a sudden noise around me. Other times I was laughing so hard. Aly upped her game in this book. I loved everything about this book.

The ending definitely was one big SHOCKER to me!! I am honestly counting down to Aly's next book. I can't give anything away. Except there's a few people who need to be fixed. They must be healed from the inside.

BROKEN COURSE-
''6 Healing Stars!!''

Aly Martinez has done it again!! She also made me go from hating a character to absolutely loving her! I mean I never hated Sarah as much as other people have, but she definitely wasn't my favorite. Leo as well I loved before. And, even now he is more sexier.

Now, before I even start I'm going to say that even though this is considered a standalone I would NOT read this book first!!

We meet Sarah in Changing Course. That book was about her husband Brett trying to save her. Sarah was in a terrible car accident that ended up killing her best friend Manda. After the accident Sarah was not the same person. She had a brain injury and was emotionally destroyed over killing her best friend. Sarah did terrible things to Brett and his new girlfriend.

Then, her story continued in Stolen Course with Caleb. He was engaged to her best friend. Caleb had it out for her. Caleb swore she killed Manda and made it his mission to destroy her. Until he met and fell in love with Sara's sister.

Leo. Oh Leo. We met him in Aly's other book Among The Ashes. He went from being an undercover police office to being in witness protection. Leo made the choice to save Erica's life. Now, they were in hiding. Until Erica fell in love. Leo and Erica's boyfriend Slate took matters in their own hands to get Erica & Leo's lives back.

Now, Leo can live his life the way he is supposed too. He opened up a bodyguard agency. Leo should be living his life. Except he still can not live with everything that had happened with Erica before he was able to save her. Leo suffers from depression & PTSD. Though you can't tell most of the time.

Fast forward 7 years and Sarah & Leo are meeting on a busy street corner and both their lives are changed. Sarah has not been with a man in 7yrs. Yet, she agreed to go out with Leo. Sarah is constantly over thinking everything. But, Leo never minds. Leo always knows what to say to Sarah to calm her down or make her see everything is going to be OK.

Then, Leo disappears for a few day and Sarah is pissed. She goes to Leo's and wasn't prepared for what she walking into. Leo in a dark bedroom laying under the covers. Leo begs her to go. But, Sarah being Sarah she doesn't go anywhere. That's when they both start to slowly realize that they were made for each other.

Both understood the other one. They knew when to push & when not to push. The things they get into are hysterical too. Overtime they learn that it's not about fixing each other. But, they are there to not let the other one fall and crumble. They seem to have things thrown in their faces just when they least expect it. But, as long as they are next to each other everything will be just fine. These 2 were also very hot! I mean their hottness was off the charts hot. *wink wink* When Leo started speaking Spanish I was done. Lol

I am very upset that this is the last book of the series. I loved all the characters from this whole series including Leo's book. And, was stoked when we got to see everybody. I was very glad with the relationship Caleb & Sarah got too. All the epilogue's where excellent as well too.

Aly left nothing open. She made you feel for Leo and Sarah and all their issues. Every time there was a flashback I found myself crying to laughing at the present. I'm really sad to say goodbye to this group of friends.
rows.title: One Of The BEST Series Out!!

rows.text:
When I was in High School my tweedy, Shakespearean teacher wrote on my Permanent Record Card that, "( I ) was obsessed with WEIRD, FANTASTIC STORIES" - not much of a favorable evaluation for a young fellow preparing for a college career in the Sciences. I learned of this comment many years later when my college advisor did me the favor of leaving his office door open one Saturday morning so that I could conveniently look up my records in his file cabinet. In High School and later into adulthood I followed up on his pronouncement and dutifully read through Burroughs, Haggard, Lovecraft, Hodgson, Hogan, Poe (Comic Books - Oooooo! Not Them!!) etc. while my science education protected me from any harm. Although I did not particularly approve of Mr. E________'s evaluation in my permanent record I followed through on his ominous assessment. Unfortunately, I did not come across Kenneth Robeson and Doc Savage until much later in life. This goes to prove that Mr. E__________ was in part wrong - A true Fantasy Fanatic would have come across Doc much earlier.

That said, with my much more modest familiarity with the fantastic I seemed to have saved some of the very best fantasy until last (I am near eighty now and have only known of Doc for the last 20 years). Of late, I came upon a small collection the 1970's Bantam paperbacks (six) by way of a young fellow known to me only as "DOMINIC" who did the favor of writing his name inside the cover of of one of the six Bantams much later deposited in Abie Baby's Used Bookstore (by his mother?) for me to find. I had previously read some of the fancy reprints (cardboard covers, illustrations and newsy bits) but Dominic's somewhat wrinkled copies with tanned pages and old pulp smell proved perfect for drawing me in - or, as Oscar Wilde once wrote, "The pathways to the soul are the senses....." Dominic apparently only had these six (and one other not included) - since he had only checked off these titles on the listing provided on the back cover and on one of the interior pages of his copy of "The Magic Island" (originally published as "Ost" - Who would want to ignore that?). Now I am reading "The Magic Island" and it is a perfect "TREASURE!!"

Of course, perhaps your High School mentor did not say you were "obsessed with the fantastic" but to appreciate my ardor for the little (suitably cheap) wrinkled (quite appropriately) copy that Dominic bequeathed to me it would not hurt you at all. In fact, it would definitely do wonders!!! I do not think that Mr. E___________ would approve "The Magic Island" as "literature" along with his moldering volumes I would peruse in his classroom cabinet during after school hours. Henry James proclaims that literature must contain characters who are "vessels of experience" and must be able to articulate the meaning of such experience for the betterment of the greater part of humanity. Doc Savage and his pals are as Monty Python's J. Cleese would put it, "... something. completely different." MONK, HAM, RENNY et. al. are idiosyncratic fellows both in personality and appearance with either strength or intelligence which comes into play in aiding Doc in solving crimes based on a few forensic hints. Their names are allegorical and their behaviors get them involved in fascinating locales and situations regardless of their family histories and "experiences" beyond those which allow them to do their work effectively as kinds of "scientists." Flaubert and Dostoevsky (among others) develop the concept that words and syntax can only create "fiction" since the "consciousness" exists outside of material reality (or only in the realm of "possibility" within the mathematical information of which the Universe is most fundamentally composed. ) Logic and philosophy exist only within the consciousness and these ideas exert no restraints on this reality. Einstein puts the matter succinctly, "The best ideas are created wholly within the human mind without reference to observable objects. " Thus, words or letters are used as symbols for unseen particulars - mass, energy, momentum and enthalpy, etc., etc. manipulated by the rules of mathematics. Poetry only among all literary techniques approaches mathematics in the manipulation of unseen particulars. The objects of Science are two fold: first the accounting for facts, and second the formulation of reliable predictions. Therefore, the terse writing of Lester Dent as the author of the Savage Sagas approaches mathematics in its simplicity and limits itself to unseen particulars manipulated by the story characters in accounting for events (facts) and making reliable predictions (what happens) as in mathematical calculations. Fiction provides a wonderful way to "rot your mind" as Harvey Kurtzman used to say in his wonderful, old ten cent MAD MAGAZINES of the day as he parodied the silly, human views of reality. Even Mr. E________ did admit (grudgingly) when I spoke on MAD in classroom "reports" that MAD did seem to "appeal to the more intelligent students!!!!" So much for "obsessed" you OLD BUZZARD!!! (He lived well in to his nineties.) Mr. E________ in a candid moment commented on a book I was reading at the time namely, "I am Legend" by Richard Matheson - "Well, this sounds pretty exciting, but you better get accustomed to reading "boring books" if you are going to college!!" Incidentally, a friend reported to me that in the holdings of an Estate Sale held at my Mentor's former home after his passing was a Doc Savage novel. A possibly spurious report since so far it has not been confirmed... One afternoon in class Mr. E_______ stood at the head of the class and in what appeared to be an epiphany he pressed the end of one temple support of his glasses against his lips and looked out a great window of the classroom into a solid brick wall of an adjacent building. Then, with a far away look he intoned: "When you are young you have dreams.....Then time passes and you wonder where your dreams have gone....." Reflecting on this statement in view of the comments made above about the fictional nature of "great literature," who was obsessed with "weird, fantastic stories"? Incidentally, Mr. E_____ thoroughly enjoyed Sheridan's 1775 vintage play, "The Rivals" where during class readings I observed his barely concealed mirth when Mrs. Malaprop came through with a "real zinger"!!! We students, however, reacted with a cavernous, "Yawn!!!" . In contrast, I spied Mr. E____ cast a grimace of disdain at the screen, when at a local movie house the audience howled in delight at the antics of the then popular Mr. Magoo.

Why did he not take the prophetic advice of the Shades of Literature when they advised:

Sophocles (speaking through Odysseus) : " I pity him in his misery for all that he is my foe, for he is bound fast to a dread doom . I think of my own lot no less than his. For I see that we are but phantoms, all we who live, or fleeting shadows..."

Yeats; "Come away , O Human child! To the waters and the wild with a faery hand in hand, for the the world's more full of weeping than you can understand...."

Poe: "All we see or seem is but a dream within a dream...."

Shelley (speaking through Ozymandias): "Look at my works, ye Mighty, and despair!"

Numerous other similar quotes could have been included, but these seem sufficient to compose a "weird, fantastic" vision of reality.

For all its lack of "literary value" THE MAGIC ISLAND has everything you might want from great writing except long paragraphs replete with unnecessary detailed descriptions, scientifically invalid facts, long passages of stream of consciousness ruminations, endless conversation and prosaic exploits of ordinary humans!!! Who needs these anyway???? Let's get down to the interesting stuff that any FANATIC is looking for!!! And best of all Kenneth Robeson will never be included in Hornstein - Percy - and Brown's "Reader's Companion to World literature (Guide to Immortal Masterpieces)" (Mentor Book, 1984). Wow!! What good does that do anyway? Make you want to wear a scratchy, tweed suit??? If Dostoevsky had written weird, pulp fiction (as of my reading I am not sure he didn't) Raskolnikov would not have borrowed an axe, spent a lengthy Siberian sojourn, and sweet, little Alyona Ivanova could have retired to a villa in the south of France. And, if Dickens had done the same, would he have needed to ridicule school officials endlessly in Oliver Twist? I could do that for him now. (More effectively? ) What the hey?! Would Leonard Zelig, and others like him have been haunted throughout life simply because they never read "Moby Dick" including its endless exposition on cetology and monomania? Psst! Psst! (Whisper...Whisper) In an introductory literature class in college we were to read the aforementioned novel. I read the Classic Comic instead and got an "A" on the test!!! I love it when that happens!!! Let's get serious here!! If High School students read more pulp fiction they would never have to say, "I haven't read anything since the first grade!!" The "Magic Island" has everything a budding Literaturist could need and more (EVEN an "A" on a book report). And, they have the wonderful property of "not being one page longer" than necessary as M. Twain once quipped. The only one who gets that kind of a grade reading literature is that funny looking kid who sat in the corner over there. He told me he has all eighty odd Dr. Savage novels and they were his favorites. So there!!!

Speaking of boring books, as a college freshmen I was saddled with reading "Crime and Punishment"!!!!! There I sat
on my sagging bunk in a dimly light dormitory hovel - cinder block walls and all, holding this turgid tome in my skinny fingers...... reading about Raskolnikov and his twisted views of reality. My friend at lunch told be he had to read "The Possessed" by the same author. This kid always developed a weird gleam in his eyes when he recounted for me the endless pages of dialog where wretches (all sharing that "Lean and hungry look" not unlike Cassius) ranted regarding "NIHILISM," "IDEALISM" "SUPER MEN" and the like. - At such times his eyes fairly glowed with fascination. (Did I use 'fair' as an adverb? How many days in Hell is that worth?} That kid was gone at the end of the first semester (needless to say). After my reading I avoided splitting firewood at home on Christmas Vacation. My father thought college had made me "effete." Dad also bought me for Christmas a fancy watch from a local Pawn Shop. NO THANKS!!

Well, if you still do not get what I am raving about, go on Amazon and buy a used copy of "The Magic Island" !!!
You will not regret it!!! Scout's Honor!!! (I was a Boy Scout for about three days, so I know about such things)!! Incidentally, if you see Dominic, thank him for me!!

"The works of the great poets have never been read by mankind, for only great poets can read them. (..........) But of reading as a noble intellectual enterprise they know little or nothing; yet this only is reading, in a high sense, not that which lulls us as a luxury and suffers the nobler faculties to sleep the while, but what we have to stand on tip-toe to read and devote our most alert and wakeful hours to."

In view of the analysis of the "noble intellectual enterprise" Thoreau might revise his admonition.

All said, I much admired Mr. E_____. For all my satire I must admit that he influenced me more that any other teacher. I did not become a literaturist, but he provided a wider lens through which to view reality. And therefore provided me with the basis of a better life. Included above I am sure are some punctuation errors (HORRORS!!!) and some ungrammatical formulations (GASP!!!).....I hope he would not have been too deeply wounded by my report to his class.

Your Old Buddy, Whizbang!!!
rows.title: A TREASURE!!!

rows.text:
PROS: IT SEARS BEEF EXCELLENT! high temps make grilling EEZYBREEZY! small size PERFECT for our small patio
CONS: ok-i hate cleaning it-but i hate cleaning EVERYTHING! wish they would have added the side burner even with this grills smaller sized grill

MY LONG REVIEW:

ok--i used to HATE grilling.

whenever we had cookouts at our place, i found the man to MAN our grill because i never could get the knack of it. with the heat fluctuating all the time, i found it stressful to grill and oftentimes would just SWOON over other's success at it---i wanted to be like THEM!!!! but no, it always happened the same way-- sometimes things turned out--sometimes they didnt (& usually DIDNT when my in-laws were over for supper! haha)--SO--plain and simple--i hated it.

with a PASSION!!

have i mentioned how much i DID NOT like grilling!!!???? hahaha!!!

since recently relocating to florida, i was NOT looking forward to getting another grill. i felt a terrible pit in my stomach just thinking about how much money we were gonna waste when we bought another grill that i was gonna watch deteriorate through my kitchen window from lack of use.

spring arrives and there we are at a favorite place of ours: LOWES.

yippeee!!! will ya just LOOK at all those shiny NEW grills!!! hubby happily drags me through the rows and rows of them and we start reading, investigating, comparing, learning.

i HAD to pay attention because by the look on my wonderful husbands face--

we were going home with a grill that day--NO DOUBT ABOUT IT.

which i thought was kind of silly because i could SEE him thinking about those grilled steaks THAT I COULD NEVER MAKE CORRECTLY--which also means EDIBLE!!!! how could he have forgotten how much i DIDNT grill before???

whatever--we were getting one--so i figured i better hunker down and really look at them.

first thing i noticed was --there are some seriously MONSTER grills out there!!!

even when we came upon the Char Broil Quantum Infrared Series, i kept thinking "too much too much!!!"

too big--i didnt want all that space! i dont even KNOW anyone in florida that we can invite over to FILL all that space up with food! and theres only me-darling hubby-and 17 yr old son!

but this Char Broil line really struck me as user friendly.

it made me imagine that even i could grill successfully.

thats when the magic happened for me.

all the way down at the end of the monsters was a very small version off the monsters.

and i mean small! rhode island could kick the crap out of it in a fight-- THATS how small i'm talking!

;-)

the side shelves folded down & created a patio version size that didnt intimidate me.

i read about all the infrared features and was getting just a little bit excited about it.

could i actually make a steak that wasn't "jerkey"? the more i pondered it, the more my husband kept commenting on how cute it was! (he doesn't use the word "cute" that often, so this was momentous to say the least) but he was right!!! it IS cute.

just enough for us.

so . . .

thats it!

we bought it. picked out a nice Char Broil cover, bought a propane tank with fuel, and were on our way home to put it together.

darling husband (dh) had that thing put together in about 1-1 1/2 hours.

he took his time and did it in FRONT of the tv!!!!

aargh!

so i had to WAIT until i could actually get it outside to season it!

i read the directions (because i'm a GIRL--THATS why!!),

then proceeded to BATHE & wipe it completely down with peanut oil.

went online to see if i couldnt find some reviews to read (because it's AFTER you buy the product that its good to know what you paid for, of course. . .i mean, we're not STUPID ya know! ahem.)
while the grill was burning off the oil--thus seasoning itself.

oh boy.

i hardly found ONE SINGLE POSITIVE review!!!!!

--complaints GALORE about it parts of the grill body rusting, the gratees were rusting, the infrared pans were clogging, yadda yadda yadda bad grill bad grill!!!

"honey? heh heh . . . these reviews dont look so good"

yeah, i got a bit nervous--but it was good that i read those reviews.

next day i bought steaks.

charbroil.com had some HANDY videos on tips & techniques and recipes and i watched that guy grill his strip steaks to perfection and went immediately to the commissary to buy OUR steaks.

ohhh i could hardly contain myself i was sooo excited.

went online again to get a great Steak Butter recipe that i could apply to the steaks to melt on them afterward while they were "resting".

guess what???

these

steaks

ROCKED!!!!!!

i am not kidding! i grilled them to perfection!!

my husband literally MOANED while he ate his and kept naming off all of our favorite steak houses that MY steaks were better than!!! hahaha!!!

i felt RIDICULOUSLY triumphant!!

im serious--honeys--you'd think i WORKED for Charb Broil!!! i was and AM STILL sooooo STOKED about this grill (no pun intended).

ive been using it nearly EVERY week since purchasing it ! !

i am getting a feel for the high temps and what they do with different types meats--and different thicknesses of meat-- and i am in hog heaven now.

seriously.

you heard it here first people---

i LOVELOVELOVE GRILLINNNNG!!!!!!!!!!

this infrared grill and the pans under them make everything sooo easy without the flare-ups that usually ruined my meals. i follow the directions for cleaning it and seasoning it everytime i use it--i dont LIKE TO CLEAN IT--but i DO IT--it doesnt take but about 5 minutes!!

those folks that have complained about what the grates "LOOKED" like--where its "rusty looking" it's actually SEASONING!!

they are SUPPOSED to be UGLY UGLY UGLY!!!

you arent supposed to clean all that black seasoning OFF of them---they arent supposed to STAY looking nice and silver!!!

so what that here and there where i havent "oiled" them there is a tad bit of surface-y rust--nothing serious--it's just the same as when you use an iron pot---ya gotta keep it oiled so it seasons properly!

it rained sideways here with a bad storm one night and water got up under our grill cover--so right on the floor where the door closes on the inside theres a spot of surface rust.

if having a perfectly beautiful looking grill is what matters to you---then this probably isnt the grill for you.

i, myself, dont care that the black paint is peeling on the inside of my grill because of the 500 degree heat!!! lol

these are things that do NOT matter to me when folks are sitting around myyyy table all moaning and humming about how good their food is!!

so--i know this is the longest review you've ever read--its actually only the second and longest one ive ever written--so im a bit on the yappy side, i KNOW!!!!

but i am one happy little puppy here on the panhandle of florida enjoying the snot outta this GREAT GRILL!!!

i needed to post this here on amazon's reviews, because i thought that maybe i could write a realistic & needed "common sense" ? review about this grill---it certainly isn't PERFECT for everyone.

but its perfect for me, and for someone that literally felt like she had the FLU whenever the subject of grilling a meal came up--- i'd say that says a lot.

i love it. so much, i would marry it.

its true!!

i actually wanna marry my grill.

heh heh heh.
rows.title: Oh how i love lthee,  let me count the ways!

rows.text:
At last! We have our very own lesbian–Game of Thrones in lesfic!! YES! Eliza Andrews‘ recently released “Princess of Dorsa,” Book 1 of the Chronicles of Dorsa, delivered just as Andrews promised when she announced that she’d be giving us lesfic readers our very own Game of Thrones (GoT)!! I was fortunate to be given the opportunity to beta-read an advance copy (Ta, Eliza!) and re-read the final version when it was out on Amazon….will be re-reading this, tbh! Anyway, Andrews, with her usual knack for weaving compelling stories with her brilliant writing style, gives Mr. Martin a run for his money. I was reeling after devouring it literally in one bated breath. I wanted MORE!!!

There was so much going on in this Book 1, I was beside meself with utter amazement and awe because I couldn’t believe I was actually reading about this whole universe that Andrews so richly created, inspired by GoT‘s, complete with the fabulous Empire consisting of the Four Realms (with the West being the largest most populated, and the East being the desert, strange and exotic, rich with myths and superstitions that the West and other Realms steered clear of), conquered by an Emperor from a Noble House, with Wise Men (like the Maesters in GoT) as the Empire’s influential advisers besides the military commanders. The Empire, as in GoT, obviously was always under threats of rebellion or attacks either from within the walls of the Realms or beyond its territories. So, Andrews’ Empire was ruled by the noble house, House of Dorsa. Princess Natasia aka Tasia was the daughter of its king, Emperor Andreth, future Empress-in-waiting. The Wise Men from the House of Wisdom, just like the Maesters from the Citadel in GoT, were an order of scholars, healers, messengers and scientists. I was utterly enthralled by Andrews’ vivid descriptions of the surroundings, the designs, the colours, the outfits of the different characters. They were visually stunning in me mind!

I was immediately intrigued by Tasia the moment she appeared on the first page of the book. Although Andrews portrayed her as the typical young and spoiled royal princess, future heiress, Empress of the Throne, who cared for nothing but herself, Tasia was also head strong, fiercely independent, smart and intelligent, with a streak of wild, cunning, mischievous and reckless behavioural traits, if only to escape the inevitability of her being married off to a worthy nobleman who’d take over her father’s position as the Emperor (as we know, even in historical fiction, men still held all the power whilst women were men’s properties), and her hatred for being imprisoned behind the royal walls because of the responsibilities that were expected of her instead of being allowed to be free to do whatever she desired. So obviously, she tried her utmost to rebel against her father and all the sodded rules. But the best thing I loved about the saga’s main protagonist, Tasia, thanks to Andrews, was that she was a LESBIAN (bloody yeh!)…even though being gay was not allowed and she had to hide it (take in male lovers, for e.g.) and be vigilant about her sexual trysts with women. But a near-success assassination attempt on her life put her constant recklessness and rule-breaking days to a screeching halt! And yes, that was when the story began.

But me favourite moment came when Josyln of Terinto, the best swordswoman in the Imperial Army, made her first appearance! She was charged as Tasia’s bodyguard, much to Tasia’s dismay because she saw Joslyn as an obstacle to her freedom to engage in rule-breaking activities. Joslyn was also expected to teach Tasia, the future ruler of the Empire, self-defence, which angered Tasia even more! Oh…I absolutely LOVED Josyln who was also a…LESBIAN!! YES! I had no words to describe just how excited I felt, really (imagining GoT but with lesbians kept playing in me mind because I was still coming to terms with the fact that me wish actually came true, thanks to Andrews!)! A strong, exotic woman hailed from the desert wasteland called Terinto in the mysterious East, with a quiet nature, intimidatingly observant, with utterly intelligent and deeply soulful eyes that held many secrets, and took her role as Tasia’s bodyguard very seriously. Andrews skilfully depicted Tasia and Josyln’s first encounter with richly described expressions of Tasia’s utter dislike for her designated “shadow” against Josyln’s seemingly unconcerned, apathetic and indifferent behavioural response, which irked Tasia even more! But their chemistry was effing palpable, bouncing off the pages like balls of smouldering flames! I was immediately enthralled. Andrews spared no effort in developing their initially fractured protector-protectee, teacher-student relationship, with grounded authenticity and wit. Oh, just wait until you read about how Josyln tried very hard to train Tasia in the art of self-defence, from the ground up! All the “harsh work” that Tasia felt was thrust upon her spoiled, taking-liberties-for-granted, utterly sheltered life, with Josyln not taking any shite from her was HILARIOUS! The progression of their relationship from enemies to friends was utterly captivating because everything was happening in the midst of all the political, power play with Tasia, her father, and his advisers, the apparently neverending war in the West, threats from the East, not to mention rumours that the banned religious sect, The Cult of Culo (something like GoT’s Lord of Light), was gaining strength and power, supernatural or otherwise! As if that wasn’t enough, Tasia was also plagued by the wretched “marriage of strategic alliance” which was expected of her. Although, ruling the Empire herself without a man beside her, wasn’t beyond the realm of possibility!

As Tasia spent more time with Josyln, observing her behaviour, her perception about her bodyguard changed little by little, from the preconceived ideas she had based on negative stereotypes instilled upon her and the rest of the so-called “civilised” West regarding people from the deserts in the East who were perceived as tribal barbarians, uncultured, uncivilised, laced with mad beliefs about the supernatural, myths and dark magic, to being enlightened by her exposure to the person and character that Joslyn was. I love how Andrews always writes about class, race, social politics in her stories, how they affect human behaviour, interactions and relationships between people from different political, social and economic status. No exception in the story about Joslyn and Tasia’s journey, as Andrews masterfully weaved the threads of their differences, slowly untangling and bonding them through all the adventures, encounters, communication that these two shared along the way.

The best thing about Andrews’ storytelling of this epic fantasy was how she merged the action-filled, thrilling adventures with the relationship between Tasia and Joslyn ever so seamlessly. This story is not focussed on romance alone, not by a long shot, but much more on the friendship these two struck with deeply felt emotions as it developed in progressive stages and the camaraderie that Tasia formed with a few of the vital characters as the story progressed into her inevitable political position of power. Joslyn became not only her protector but her close, protective friend and trusted adviser. I was gratified by the fact that Andrews spent the time to develop their relationship before it gradually turned into something more intimate. It was glorious to uncover their growing affection for each other because the pacing was utterly organic and perfectly timed in conjunction with the wretched journey they had to tread on as the political turmoil within the Realms became more dangerous and threatening to the future of the House Dorsa. In other words, the slow-burn type crafted between Tasia and Joslyn was ideally perfect! It was intoxicating to read their slow but utterly affective development.

Just like GoT, amidst all the political manoeuvring amongst the cast of characters plotting against each other in order to gain power for control of the Empire, there were some absolutely mindfucking twists and turns! The dangerous game of chess reached its peak when Tasia, Joslyn and her guards were confronted by faceless enemies and strange mythical creatures just when they thought they were in safe waters. Andrews’ brilliant depiction of the non-stop, violent, gruesome action sequences was nothing short of EPIC! I was literally gasping for breath by the time the big explosive scene came crashing, involving Joslyn’s mad, heroic stance and Tasia’s courageous move as a desperate measure, as they encountered the entity! Phew! This insidious, dark, supernatural entity that Andrews crafted into this series was phenomenal. I was entranced. I know it will play a vital role as the saga continues in future installments of this epic fantasy series! Nevermind that there weren’t any dragons because this malevolent entity (god knows how long its rein stretches as I’m sure subsequent installments of the Series will reveal!) consumed me sense of intrigue and curiosity completely! It was simply magnificent how Andrews wrote it so vividly I could visually see the entire scene unfold before me mind’s eye! Truly monumental!

As Tasia and Joslyn journeyed deeper into the pit of political turmoil, not surprisingly, nothing was what it seemed. Let’s just say that shit hit the fan as the power play became more deadly than Tasia and Joslyn would have wanted. Needless to say, the final action sequence was a pulse-racing, bile-inducing, heart-pounding event! What a 3rd and final act, indeed! Bravo, Andrews! As we know how the political game is played, unexpected cast of characters emerged showing their true colours as Tasia and Co. fought to survive. And so was Joslyn who was mercilessly separated from Tasia! Ohhh…just wait until you read how Tasia was handling the dilemma, the deeply held emotions and the depth of her feelings for her loyal and trusted friend and confidant, adviser, protector who came to hold the most important place in her heart! Andrews’ superbly written depiction of Tasia’s inner psyche, her emotional state about Josyln, especially upon uncovering just how Josyln felt about her which was left unspoken, was heartrendingly affective. On the other hand, Joslyn’s fierce and steadfast love, loyalty and protectiveness of Tasia reverberated powerfully as Andrews’ poetically resonating words and expressions seeped through the recesses of me mind ever so effectively. Phew!

Oh, when you read this book and come to the turning point at the end of the final act, you’ll grasp at all signs of hope and will for Joslyn and Tasia to triumph! How they were gonna go about it, I was left reeling because Andrews has this knack for throwing breadcrumbs that would leave you wanting more, yearning for more! Needless to say, the cliffhanger left me clutching the edge of me seat, screaming, demanding for the next installment to be available so I could learn Joslyn’s plan to reunite with Tasia, who’s become one of me favourite fictional characters! What’s more, Joslyn’s character throughout this installment remained hugely a mystery. She was an enigma, really. And because Andrews portrayed her with such deep-seated resonance, I was desperate to know more about her, to discover what her back story was, what made her who she was! Alas, Andrews only revealed bits of her heartbreaking history, which already had me wanting to just shield this brave tortured soul from further misfortune, misery and sorrow, who deserves nothing but happiness and joy in her life from here on out! I want to see Tasia love and protect her at all costs throughout the Series! Ohhhhh….!!!!

Anyway, the saga continues in the next installment, roughly entitled, “Soldier of Dorsa,” which hopefully will be available mid-2019!! Andrews promised the 2nd installment will reveal a whole lot more about Joslyn!! Bloody hell YES! Gaaaaahhh!!! The wait is KILLING me!!! And yes, besides that, I’m utterly psyched to also find out more about the other corners of the Realms, other groups and regions, especially the magical, mystical part of the universe, how it’s gonna play a role in Tasia and Joslyn’s continued journey.

All in all, as if you haven’t realised already from me aforementioned commentary, “Princess of Dorsa” is a RESOUNDING MUST-READ for everyone! In particular, if you’re a fan of epic fantasy fiction and/or simply the TV adaptation of GoT, if not the original “A Song of Ice and Fire” by the one and only George R.R. Martin, you’ll find this book a heart-pounding, breathtaking, utterly suspenseful, fantastic read!! It’s defo reread material for me! I HIGHLY RECOMMEND everyone take this INCREDIBLE RIDE of an EPIC saga that is the best fantasy series I’ve read besides Martin’s “A Song Of Ice and Fire” series! More imperatively, Andrews’ Chronicles of Dorsa, starting with this Book 1 is SO MUCH MORE and BETTER because…LESBIANS!!!!! Bloody hell YES! This book has become one of me favourites not only in 2018 but in the world of fiction and I know, just like I know about Jody Klaire’s Above and Beyond series, the entire Chronicles of Dorsa series, regardless of how many installments there will be, also makes me list of all-time favourites in general and lesbian fiction!

ONE-CLICK it. READ it. RIDE it. EXPERIENCE it. FEEL it. REVEL in it. REPEAT.

Obligatory mention: I received an advanced (beta) copy in exchange for an honest review (commentary, really!)
rows.title: A RESOUNDING MUST-READ EPIC Fantasy Saga that Will Leave You Reeling & Desperate for Book 2!

rows.text:
Book 1:
Oh my god. I immediately fell in love with Secrets. Just like all of Ella Steele's novels. Anna is an amazing character to read. I complete fell in love with her personality. She's gutsy, witty, and an amazing roll model of sorts. She knows what she wants and goes after it. She wants to be a wedding photographer. She ended up working for Cole Stevens. A broken man who photographs hottie women in sexy underwear. While at first Anna thinks she'll hate working for him, she finds the job compelling, and changing everything she believed in.

Honestly a need to read!!!

Favorite Quote:I honestly loved this scene with Anna's parents. I swear to goodness they were hysterical.
Cole lifts his glass to take a sip when my mom says, "it's not good for a pregnant woman to be so skinny. You remember that Cole. Fatten her up before you knock her up."

Book 2:
So I decided half way through Secrets Vol. 1 that I needed to buy Vol. 2. Boy oh boy was I right. I swear Secrets is filled with so much sexual tension, I never know what's going to happen.

During Secrets Vol. 2 Anna's attraction to Cole skyrockets. She can't get enough of just being with him. While she thinks he feels the same way she isn't sure. Which makes her second guess everything about herself. Then he offers her a job. A job that she doesn't think she wants, a job that she thinks is demeaning to everything she stands for. Until Cole shows her differently, after he does she wants to everything about his artistic style, by delving into his world all the more, making him open up. Cole and Anna still struggle with their attraction to one another, and while she's willing to do anything and everything for him, he continues to shut he down. Anna doesn't know why he keeps shutting her down again and again, but she knows something is going on, and she needs to find out what.

Honestly I could not put this book down and read it in one sitting. It's riveting, and compelling, and will have you at the end of your seat even at the last page. Hope you pick up your copies!!!

Book 3:
Okay seriously that ending!!!!! Let's just say there was a lot of swearing going on when I read the ending to Vol. 3. Ugh I swear I was so not expecting what happened!! Which was awesome because I hate when I know what happens in the end, but still. Oh my goodness I neeeeeeed to know what is going to happen next. Like NOOOOOW!!!!!

Vol. 3 gives a teeny taste of Cole and his naughty side, which was awesome because he is freaking sexy. I love Cole, he definitely has issues which I think makes him all the more attractive.

Things are not looking good for Anna however, everything is looking worse. She's falling head over heals for Cole and no matter what she does, she can't get him off her mind. Not even when a new guy shows up, a guy who's willing to be everything she every wanted, except he's not Cole. While working at her internship she gets a call from the deans office that there was a problem. Now she has to find a new internship or she won't graduate. Everything spirals out of Anna's control, until the very last heart breaking second.

PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE, Holly. Take pity on my soul and produce the next Secrets short story. I am dying over here. If you could take pity on me and feed my addiction I would be forever in your debt. 8)

Favorite Quote: This scene made me giggle, as did many in this short novel but this one was my favorite.
"I sigh. "You sure you don't want to be rebound guy? All sex, no string attached?"
He laughs, "You don't want a rebound guy. They have herpes."

Book 4:
I received an ARC of Secrets for a honest review! Happy dance for the ARC from H.M. Ward I am so so so happy and excited I got one! Yay me!!!

Holy bleeeep!!!! Ah so I honestly have no and so many words for this book. Ah!!! So freaking awesome!!! And that ending....Alright let me start with the beginning and not the end.

Secrets Vol. 4 picks up right where vol. 3 left off. Anna is at the beach with Cole and starts to leave heart broken and angry. Getting on her bike it the pouring rain she takes off and then gets into an accident her bike skidding and dragging Anna with it. Cole helping her up and starts to argue with her. Anna upset, in pain tells him off and then takes off towards her parents.

When reaching her parents she gets cleaned up she explains what happened and then Anna and her father have an epic talk. I was honestly blown away by this talk it was so inspirational I was feeling a little pumped up myself:

“The ones we love are worth fighting for. Me and you are fighters, Anna. We don’t let things slip between our fingers, unless we want them to.”

After Anna's talk with her parents Anna goes home the next day with a game plan. I have to say I loved what she did next hehe not going to tell what it is but it is EPIC and completely and totally awesomesauce! No lie, when I was reading it I was laughing so hysterically. Hehe so funny. Anway after the epicness Anna is served a court order that she has to show up for the Sottero vs. Stevens case. From there Anna starts taking control of her life, trying to figure out what she wants to do next for her career and her love life.
